  51 |     _mistral_import_error_msg = (
  52 |         "Mistral format requires `mistral-common` to be installed. Please run "
  53 |         "`pip install mistral-common[image,audio]` to install it."
  54 |     )
  55 | 
  56 | 
  57 | logger = logging.getLogger("hf-to-gguf")
  58 | 
  59 | 
  60 | ###### MODEL DEFINITIONS ######
  61 | 
  62 | class SentencePieceTokenTypes(IntEnum):
  63 |     NORMAL = 1
  64 |     UNKNOWN = 2
  65 |     CONTROL = 3
  66 |     USER_DEFINED = 4
  67 |     UNUSED = 5
  68 |     BYTE = 6
  69 | 
  70 | 
  71 | class ModelType(IntEnum):
  72 |     TEXT = 1
  73 |     MMPROJ = 2
  74 | 
  75 | 
  76 | AnyModel = TypeVar("AnyModel", bound="type[ModelBase]")
  77 | 
  78 | 
  79 | class ModelBase:
  80 |     _model_classes: dict[ModelType, dict[str, type[ModelBase]]] = {
  81 |         ModelType.TEXT: {},
  82 |         ModelType.MMPROJ: {},
  83 |     }
  84 | 
  85 |     dir_model: Path
  86 |     ftype: gguf.LlamaFileType
  87 |     fname_out: Path
  88 |     is_big_endian: bool
  89 |     endianess: gguf.GGUFEndian
  90 |     use_temp_file: bool
  91 |     lazy: bool
  92 |     dry_run: bool
  93 |     hparams: dict[str, Any]
  94 |     model_tensors: dict[str, Callable[[], Tensor]]
  95 |     gguf_writer: gguf.GGUFWriter
  96 |     model_name: str | None
  97 |     metadata_override: Path | None
  98 |     dir_model_card: Path
  99 |     remote_hf_model_id: str | None
 100 | 
 101 |     # subclasses should define this!
 102 |     model_arch: gguf.MODEL_ARCH
 103 | 
 104 |     # subclasses should initialize this!
 105 |     block_count: int
 106 |     tensor_map: gguf.TensorNameMap
 107 | 
 108 |     # Mistral format specifics
 109 |     is_mistral_format: bool = False
 110 |     disable_mistral_community_chat_template: bool = False
 111 |     sentence_transformers_dense_modules: bool = False
 112 | 
 113 |     def __init__(self, dir_model: Path, ftype: gguf.LlamaFileType, fname_out: Path, *, is_big_endian: bool = False,
 114 |                  use_temp_file: bool = False, eager: bool = False,
 115 |                  metadata_override: Path | None = None, model_name: str | None = None,
 116 |                  split_max_tensors: int = 0, split_max_size: int = 0, dry_run: bool = False,
 117 |                  small_first_shard: bool = False, hparams: dict[str, Any] | None = None, remote_hf_model_id: str | None = None,
 118 |                  disable_mistral_community_chat_template: bool = False,
 119 |                  sentence_transformers_dense_modules: bool = False,
 120 |                  fuse_gate_up_exps: bool = False,
 121 |                  alignment: int = gguf.GGUF_DEFAULT_ALIGNMENT, sort_tensors: bool = False):
 122 |         if type(self) is ModelBase or \
 123 |                 type(self) is TextModel or \
 124 |                 type(self) is MmprojModel:
 125 |             raise TypeError(f"{type(self).__name__!r} should not be directly instantiated")
 126 | 
 127 |         if self.is_mistral_format and not _mistral_common_installed:
 128 |             raise ImportError(_mistral_import_error_msg)
 129 | 
 130 |         self.dir_model = dir_model
 131 |         self.ftype = ftype
 132 |         self.fname_out = fname_out
 133 |         self.is_big_endian = is_big_endian
 134 |         self.endianess = gguf.GGUFEndian.BIG if is_big_endian else gguf.GGUFEndian.LITTLE
 135 |         self.use_temp_file = use_temp_file
 136 |         self.lazy = not eager or (remote_hf_model_id is not None)
 137 |         self.dry_run = dry_run
 138 |         self.remote_hf_model_id = remote_hf_model_id
 139 |         self.sentence_transformers_dense_modules = sentence_transformers_dense_modules
 140 |         self.fuse_gate_up_exps = fuse_gate_up_exps
 141 |         self.sort_tensors = sort_tensors
 142 |         self._gate_exp_buffer: dict[int, Tensor] = {}
 143 |         self._up_exp_buffer: dict[int, Tensor] = {}
 144 |         self.hparams = ModelBase.load_hparams(self.dir_model, self.is_mistral_format) if hparams is None else hparams
 145 |         self.model_tensors = self.index_tensors(remote_hf_model_id=remote_hf_model_id)
 146 |         self.metadata_override = metadata_override
 147 |         self.model_name = model_name
 148 |         self.dir_model_card = dir_model  # overridden in convert_lora_to_gguf.py
 149 |         self._is_nvfp4 = False
 150 |         self._is_mxfp4 = False
 151 | 
 152 |         # Apply heuristics to figure out typical tensor encoding based on first tensor's dtype
 153 |         # NOTE: can't use field "torch_dtype" in config.json, because some finetunes lie.
 154 |         if self.ftype == gguf.LlamaFileType.GUESSED:
 155 |             for _, tensor in self.get_tensors():
 156 |                 if tensor.dim() < 2:
 157 |                     continue
 158 | 
 159 |                 if tensor.dtype == torch.bfloat16:
 160 |                     self.ftype = gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_BF16
 161 |                     logger.info("heuristics detected bfloat16 tensor dtype, setting --outtype bf16")
 162 |                     break
 163 |                 elif tensor.dtype == torch.float16:
 164 |                     self.ftype = gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_F16
 165 |                     logger.info("heuristics detected float16 tensor dtype, setting --outtype f16")
 166 |                     break
 167 |             else:
 168 |                 self.ftype = gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_F16
 169 |                 logger.info("heuristics unable to detect tensor dtype, defaulting to --outtype f16")
 170 | 
 171 |         # Configure GGUF Writer
 172 |         self.gguf_writer = gguf.GGUFWriter(path=None, arch=gguf.MODEL_ARCH_NAMES[self.model_arch], endianess=self.endianess, use_temp_file=self.use_temp_file,
 173 |                                            split_max_tensors=split_max_tensors, split_max_size=split_max_size, dry_run=dry_run, small_first_shard=small_first_shard)
 174 |         self.gguf_writer.add_custom_alignment(alignment)
 175 |         logger.info(f"aligning tensors to {alignment} bytes")
 176 |         if self.sort_tensors:
 177 |             logger.info("sorting tensors")
 178 |         else:
 179 |             logger.info("not sorting tensors")
 180 | 
 181 |         # Mistral specific
 182 |         self.disable_mistral_community_chat_template = disable_mistral_community_chat_template
 183 | 
 184 |     @classmethod
 185 |     def add_prefix_to_filename(cls, path: Path, prefix: str) -> Path:
 186 |         stem, suffix = path.stem, path.suffix
 187 |         new_name = f"{prefix}{stem}{suffix}"
 188 |         return path.with_name(new_name)
 189 | 
 190 |     def find_hparam(self, keys: Iterable[str], optional: bool = False) -> Any:
 191 |         key = next((k for k in keys if k in self.hparams), None)
 192 |         if key is not None:
 193 |             return self.hparams[key]
 194 |         if optional:
 195 |             return None
 196 |         raise KeyError(f"could not find any of: {keys}")
 197 | 
 198 |     def index_tensors(self, remote_hf_model_id: str | None = None) -> dict[str, Callable[[], Tensor]]:
 199 |         tensors: dict[str, Callable[[], Tensor]] = {}
 200 | 
 201 |         if remote_hf_model_id is not None:
 202 |             is_safetensors = True
 203 | 
 204 |             logger.info(f"Using remote model with HuggingFace id: {remote_hf_model_id}")
 205 |             remote_tensors = gguf.utility.SafetensorRemote.get_list_tensors_hf_model(remote_hf_model_id)
 206 |             for name, remote_tensor in remote_tensors.items():
 207 |                 tensors[name] = lambda r=remote_tensor: LazyTorchTensor.from_remote_tensor(r)
 208 | 
 209 |             return tensors
 210 | 
 211 |         prefix = "model" if not self.is_mistral_format else "consolidated"
 212 |         part_names: list[str] = ModelBase.get_model_part_names(self.dir_model, prefix, ".safetensors")
 213 |         is_safetensors: bool = len(part_names) > 0
 214 |         if not is_safetensors:
 215 |             part_names = ModelBase.get_model_part_names(self.dir_model, "pytorch_model", ".bin")
 216 | 
 217 |         tensor_names_from_index: set[str] = set()
 218 | 
 219 |         if not self.is_mistral_format:
 220 |             index_name = "model.safetensors" if is_safetensors else "pytorch_model.bin"
 221 |             index_name += ".index.json"
 222 |             index_file = self.dir_model / index_name
 223 | 
 224 |             if index_file.is_file():
 225 |                 logger.info(f"gguf: loading model weight map from '{index_name}'")
 226 |                 with open(index_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
 227 |                     index: dict[str, Any] = json.load(f)
 228 |                     weight_map = index.get("weight_map")
 229 |                     if weight_map is None or not isinstance(weight_map, dict):
 230 |                         raise ValueError(f"Can't load 'weight_map' from {index_name!r}")
 231 |                     tensor_names_from_index.update(weight_map.keys())
 232 |                     part_dict: dict[str, None] = dict.fromkeys(weight_map.values(), None) # ty: ignore[invalid-assignment]
 233 |                     part_names = sorted(part_dict.keys())
 234 |             else:
 235 |                 weight_map = {}
 236 |         else:
 237 |             weight_map = {}
 238 | 
 239 |         for part_name in part_names:
 240 |             logger.info(f"gguf: indexing model part '{part_name}'")
 241 |             ctx: ContextManager[Any]
 242 |             if is_safetensors:
 243 |                 ctx = cast(ContextManager[Any], gguf.utility.SafetensorsLocal(self.dir_model / part_name))
 244 |             else:
 245 |                 ctx = contextlib.nullcontext(torch.load(str(self.dir_model / part_name), map_location="cpu", mmap=True, weights_only=True))
 246 | 
 247 |             with ctx as model_part:
 248 |                 assert model_part is not None
 249 | 
 250 |                 for name in model_part.keys():
 251 |                     if is_safetensors:
 252 |                         data: gguf.utility.LocalTensor = model_part[name]
 253 |                         if self.lazy:
 254 |                             data_gen = lambda data=data: LazyTorchTensor.from_local_tensor(data)  # noqa: E731
 255 |                         else:
 256 |                             dtype = LazyTorchTensor._dtype_str_map[data.dtype]
 257 |                             data_gen = lambda data=data, dtype=dtype: torch.from_numpy(data.mmap_bytes()).view(dtype).reshape(data.shape)  # noqa: E731
 258 |                     else:
 259 |                         data_torch: Tensor = model_part[name]
 260 |                         if self.lazy:
 261 |                             data_gen = lambda data=data_torch: LazyTorchTensor.from_eager(data)  # noqa: E731
 262 |                         else:
 263 |                             data_gen = lambda data=data_torch: data  # noqa: E731
 264 |                     tensors[name] = data_gen
 265 | 
 266 |         # verify tensor name presence and identify potentially missing files
 267 |         if len(tensor_names_from_index) > 0:
 268 |             tensor_names_from_parts = set(tensors.keys())
 269 |             if len(tensor_names_from_parts.symmetric_difference(tensor_names_from_index)) > 0:
 270 |                 missing = sorted(tensor_names_from_index.difference(tensor_names_from_parts))
 271 |                 extra = sorted(tensor_names_from_parts.difference(tensor_names_from_index))
 272 |                 missing_files = sorted(set(weight_map[n] for n in missing if n in weight_map))
 273 |                 if len(extra) == 0 and len(missing_files) > 0:
 274 |                     raise ValueError(f"Missing or incomplete model files: {missing_files}\n"
 275 |                                      f"Missing tensors: {missing}")
 276 |                 else:
 277 |                     raise ValueError("Mismatch between weight map and model parts for tensor names:\n"
 278 |                                      f"Missing tensors: {missing}\n"
 279 |                                      f"Extra tensors: {extra}")
 280 | 
 281 |         return tensors
 282 | 
 283 |     @staticmethod
 284 |     def _scale_is_trivial(scale: Tensor) -> bool:
 285 |         return scale.numel() <= 1 and abs(float(scale.float().sum()) - 1.0) < 1e-6
 286 | 
 287 |     def _write_scale_tensor(self, scale_name: str, scale: Tensor):
 288 |         if not self._scale_is_trivial(scale):
 289 |             scale_f32 = scale.float().numpy().flatten()
 290 |             logger.info(f"  + {scale_name} (per-tensor scale, shape [{scale_f32.size}])")
 291 |             self.gguf_writer.add_tensor(scale_name, scale_f32)
 292 | 
 293 |     def _write_scales_tensor(self, scale_name: str, scales: list[float]):
 294 |         if not np.allclose(scales, 1.0, atol=1e-6):
 295 |             scale_vals = np.array(scales, dtype=np.float32)
 296 |             logger.info(f"  + {scale_name} (per-expert scale, shape [{len(scales)}])")
 297 |             self.gguf_writer.add_tensor(scale_name, scale_vals)
 298 | 
 299 |     def dequant_model(self):
 300 |         # If all quantized tensors were already handled (e.g. pure NVFP4), skip
 301 |         if self._is_nvfp4 and not any(k.endswith((".weight_scale", ".weight_scale_inv")) for k in self.model_tensors):
 302 |             return
 303 | 
 304 |         tensors_to_remove: list[str] = []
 305 |         new_tensors: dict[str, Callable[[], Tensor]] = {}
 306 | 
 307 |         if (quant_config := self.hparams.get("quantization_config")) and isinstance(quant_config, dict):
 308 |             quant_method = quant_config.get("quant_method")
 309 | 
 310 |             def dequant_bitnet(weight: Tensor, scale: Tensor) -> Tensor:
 311 |                 weight = weight.view(torch.uint8)
 312 |                 orig_shape = weight.shape
 313 | 
 314 |                 shift = torch.tensor([0, 2, 4, 6], dtype=torch.uint8).reshape((4, *(1 for _ in range(len(orig_shape)))))
 315 |                 data = weight.unsqueeze(0).expand((4, *orig_shape)) >> shift
 316 |                 data = data & 3
 317 |                 data = (data.float() - 1).reshape((orig_shape[0] * 4, *orig_shape[1:]))
 318 | 
 319 |                 # The scale is inverted
 320 |                 return data / scale.float()
 321 | 
 322 |             def dequant_simple(weight: Tensor, scale: Tensor, block_size: Sequence[int] | None = None) -> Tensor:
 323 |                 scale = scale.float()
 324 | 
 325 |                 if block_size is not None:
 326 |                     dim_offset = scale.ndim - len(block_size)
 327 |                     for i, size in enumerate(block_size):
 328 |                         scale = scale.repeat_interleave(size, dim_offset + i)
 329 |                     # unpad the scale (e.g. when the tensor size isn't a multiple of the block size)
 330 |                     scale = scale[tuple(slice(0, size) for size in weight.shape)]
 331 | 
 332 |                 # align scale dims to weight for correct broadcasting (e.g. [128] -> [128, 1, 1])
 333 |                 while scale.ndim < weight.ndim:
 334 |                     scale = scale.unsqueeze(-1)
 335 | 
 336 |                 return weight.float() * scale
 337 | 
 338 |             # ref: https://github.com/ModelCloud/GPTQModel/blob/037c5c0f6c9e33c500d975b038d02e7ca437546d/gptqmodel/nn_modules/qlinear/__init__.py#L437-L476
 339 |             def dequant_gptq(g_idx: Tensor, qweight: Tensor, qzeros: Tensor, scales: Tensor) -> Tensor:
 340 |                 bits = quant_config["bits"]
 341 |                 assert bits in (2, 3, 4, 8)
 342 |                 assert qweight.dtype == qzeros.dtype
 343 |                 maxq = (2 ** bits) - 1
 344 |                 weight = None
 345 |                 zeros = None
 346 |                 pack_dtype_bits = qweight.dtype.itemsize * 8
 347 | 
 348 |                 if bits in [2, 4, 8]:
 349 |                     pack_factor = pack_dtype_bits // bits
 350 |                     wf = torch.tensor(list(range(0, pack_dtype_bits, bits)), dtype=torch.int32).unsqueeze(0)
 351 |                     if self.lazy:
 352 |                         wf = LazyTorchTensor.from_eager(wf)
 353 | 
 354 |                     zeros = torch.bitwise_right_shift(
 355 |                         qzeros.unsqueeze(2).expand(-1, -1, pack_factor),
 356 |                         wf.unsqueeze(0)
 357 |                     ).to(torch.int16 if bits == 8 else torch.int8)
 358 |                     zeros = torch.bitwise_and(zeros, maxq).reshape(scales.shape)
 359 | 
 360 |                     weight = torch.bitwise_and(
 361 |                         torch.bitwise_right_shift(
 362 |                             qweight.unsqueeze(1).expand(-1, pack_factor, -1),
 363 |                             wf.unsqueeze(-1)
 364 |                         ).to(torch.int16 if bits == 8 else torch.int8),
 365 |                         maxq
 366 |                     )
 367 |                 elif bits == 3:
 368 |                     raise NotImplementedError("3-bit gptq dequantization is not yet implemented")
 369 | 
 370 |                 assert weight is not None
 371 |                 assert zeros is not None
 372 | 
 373 |                 weight = weight.reshape(weight.shape[0] * weight.shape[1], weight.shape[2])
 374 | 
 375 |                 # gptq_v2 doesn't need to offset zeros
 376 |                 if quant_config.get("checkpoint_format", "gptq") == "gptq":
 377 |                     zeros += 1
 378 | 
 379 |                 return (scales[g_idx].float() * (weight - zeros[g_idx]).float()).T
 380 | 
 381 |             def dequant_packed(w: Tensor, scale: Tensor, shape_tensor: Tensor, zero_point: Tensor | None, num_bits: int, group_size: int):
 382 |                 assert w.dtype == torch.int32
 383 |                 shape = tuple(shape_tensor.tolist())
 384 |                 assert len(shape) == 2
 385 |                 mask = (1 << num_bits) - 1
 386 | 
 387 |                 shifts = torch.arange(0, 32 - (num_bits - 1), num_bits, dtype=torch.int32)
 388 |                 if self.lazy:
 389 |                     shifts = LazyTorchTensor.from_eager(shifts)
 390 | 
 391 |                 if zero_point is None:
 392 |                     offset = 1 << (num_bits - 1)
 393 |                 else:
 394 |                     assert len(zero_point.shape) == 2
 395 |                     offset = (zero_point.unsqueeze(1) >> shifts.reshape(1, -1, 1)) & mask
 396 |                     offset = offset.reshape(-1, zero_point.shape[1])
 397 |                     # trim padding, and prepare for broadcast
 398 |                     # NOTE: the zero-point is packed along dim 0
 399 |                     offset = offset[:shape[0], :].unsqueeze(-1)
 400 | 
 401 |                 # extract values
 402 |                 # NOTE: the weights are packed along dim 1
 403 |                 unpacked = (w.unsqueeze(-1) >> shifts.reshape(1, 1, -1)) & mask
 404 |                 unpacked = unpacked.reshape(shape[0], -1)
 405 | 
 406 |                 # trim padding
 407 |                 unpacked = unpacked[:, :shape[1]]
 408 | 
 409 |                 # prepare for broadcast of the scale
 410 |                 unpacked = unpacked.reshape(shape[0], (unpacked.shape[-1] + group_size - 1) // group_size, group_size)
 411 |                 unpacked = unpacked - offset
 412 | 
 413 |                 return (unpacked * scale.unsqueeze(-1).float()).reshape(shape)
 414 | 
 415 |             if quant_method == "bitnet":
 416 |                 for name in self.model_tensors.keys():
 417 |                     if name.endswith(".weight_scale"):
 418 |                         weight_name = name.removesuffix("_scale")
 419 |                         w = self.model_tensors[weight_name]
 420 |                         s = self.model_tensors[name]
 421 |                         self.model_tensors[weight_name] = lambda w=w, s=s: dequant_bitnet(w(), s())
 422 |                         tensors_to_remove.append(name)
 423 |             elif quant_method == "fp8":
 424 |                 block_size = quant_config.get("weight_block_size")
 425 |                 for name in self.model_tensors.keys():
 426 |                     if name.endswith("_scale_inv"):
 427 |                         weight_name = name.removesuffix("_scale_inv")
 428 |                         w = self.model_tensors[weight_name]
 429 |                         s = self.model_tensors[name]
 430 |                         self.model_tensors[weight_name] = lambda w=w, s=s, bs=block_size: dequant_simple(w(), s(), bs)
 431 |                         tensors_to_remove.append(name)
 432 |                     if name.endswith(".activation_scale"):  # unused
 433 |                         tensors_to_remove.append(name)
 434 |                     if name.endswith("_activation_scale"):  # Mistral-Small-4-119B-2602, unused
 435 |                         tensors_to_remove.append(name)
 436 |                     # mistral format
 437 |                     if name.endswith(".qscale_weight"):
 438 |                         weight_name = name.removesuffix("qscale_weight") + "weight"
 439 |                         w = self.model_tensors[weight_name]
 440 |                         s = self.model_tensors[name]
 441 |                         self.model_tensors[weight_name] = lambda w=w, s=s, bs=block_size: dequant_simple(w(), s(), bs)
 442 |                         tensors_to_remove.append(name)
 443 |                     if name.endswith(".qscale_act"):
 444 |                         tensors_to_remove.append(name)
 445 |             elif quant_method == "gptq":
 446 |                 for name in self.model_tensors.keys():
 447 |                     if name.endswith(".qweight"):
 448 |                         base_name = name.removesuffix(".qweight")
 449 |                         g_idx = self.model_tensors[base_name + ".g_idx"]
 450 |                         qweight = self.model_tensors[base_name + ".qweight"]
 451 |                         qzeros = self.model_tensors[base_name + ".qzeros"]
 452 |                         scales = self.model_tensors[base_name + ".scales"]
 453 |                         new_tensors[base_name + ".weight"] = (
 454 |                             lambda g=g_idx, z=qzeros, w=qweight, s=scales: dequant_gptq(
 455 |                                 g(), w(), z(), s()
 456 |                             )
 457 |                         )
 458 |                         tensors_to_remove += [
 459 |                             base_name + n
 460 |                             for n in (
 461 |                                 ".g_idx",
 462 |                                 ".qzeros",
 463 |                                 ".qweight",
 464 |                                 ".scales",
 465 |                             )
 466 |                         ]
 467 |             elif quant_method == "compressed-tensors":
 468 |                 quant_format = quant_config["format"]
 469 |                 groups = quant_config["config_groups"]
 470 |                 if len(groups) > 1:
 471 |                     raise NotImplementedError("Can't handle multiple config groups for compressed-tensors yet")
 472 |                 weight_config = tuple(groups.values())[0]["weights"]
 473 | 
 474 |                 if quant_format == "float-quantized" or quant_format == "int-quantized" or quant_format == "naive-quantized":
 475 |                     block_size = weight_config.get("block_structure", None)
 476 |                     strategy = weight_config.get("strategy")
 477 |                     assert strategy == "channel" or strategy == "block"
 478 |                     assert weight_config.get("group_size") is None  # didn't find a model using this yet
 479 |                     for name in self.model_tensors.keys():
 480 |                         if name.endswith(".weight_scale"):
 481 |                             weight_name = name.removesuffix("_scale")
 482 |                             w = self.model_tensors[weight_name]
 483 |                             s = self.model_tensors[name]
 484 |                             self.model_tensors[weight_name] = lambda w=w, s=s: dequant_simple(w(), s(), block_size)
 485 |                             tensors_to_remove.append(name)
 486 |                 elif quant_format == "pack-quantized":
 487 |                     assert weight_config.get("strategy") == "group"
 488 |                     assert weight_config.get("type", "int") == "int"
 489 |                     num_bits = weight_config.get("num_bits")
 490 |                     group_size = weight_config.get("group_size")
 491 |                     assert isinstance(num_bits, int)
 492 |                     assert isinstance(group_size, int)
 493 |                     for name in self.model_tensors.keys():
 494 |                         if name.endswith(".weight_packed"):
 495 |                             base_name = name.removesuffix("_packed")
 496 |                             w = self.model_tensors[name]
 497 |                             scale = self.model_tensors[base_name + "_scale"]
 498 |                             shape = self.model_tensors[base_name + "_shape"]
 499 |                             zero_point = self.model_tensors.get(base_name + "_zero_point", lambda: None)
 500 |                             new_tensors[base_name] = (
 501 |                                 lambda w=w, scale=scale, shape=shape, zero_point=zero_point: dequant_packed(
 502 |                                     w(), scale(), shape(), zero_point(), num_bits, group_size,
 503 |                                 )
 504 |                             )
 505 |                             tensors_to_remove += [base_name + n for n in ("_packed", "_shape", "_scale")]
 506 |                             if (base_name + "_zero_point") in self.model_tensors:
 507 |                                 tensors_to_remove.append(base_name + "_zero_point")
 508 |                 else:
 509 |                     raise NotImplementedError(f"Quant format {quant_format!r} for method {quant_method!r} is not yet supported")
 510 |             elif quant_method == "modelopt":
 511 |                 # Mixed-precision ModelOpt models: NVFP4 tensors are handled by
 512 |                 # _generate_nvfp4_tensors; FP8 tensors have 1D weight_scale and
 513 |                 # are dequantized here. k/v scale tensors are unused.
 514 |                 for name in self.model_tensors.keys():
 515 |                     if name.endswith(".weight_scale"):
 516 |                         weight_name = name.removesuffix("_scale")
 517 |                         w = self.model_tensors[weight_name]
 518 |                         s = self.model_tensors[name]
 519 |                         self.model_tensors[weight_name] = lambda w=w, s=s: dequant_simple(w(), s(), None)
 520 |                         tensors_to_remove.append(name)
 521 |                     if name.endswith((".input_scale", ".k_scale", ".v_scale")):
 522 |                         tensors_to_remove.append(name)
 523 |             elif quant_method is not None:
 524 |                 raise NotImplementedError(f"Quant method is not yet supported: {quant_method!r}")
 525 | 
 526 |         for name in tensors_to_remove:
 527 |             if name in self.model_tensors:
 528 |                 del self.model_tensors[name]
 529 | 
 530 |         for name, value in new_tensors.items():
 531 |             self.model_tensors[name] = value
 532 | 
 533 |     def get_tensors(self) -> Iterator[tuple[str, Tensor]]:
 534 |         for name, gen in self.model_tensors.items():
 535 |             yield name, gen()
 536 | 
 537 |     def format_tensor_name(self, key: gguf.MODEL_TENSOR, bid: int | None = None, suffix: str = ".weight") -> str:
 538 |         if key not in gguf.MODEL_TENSORS[self.model_arch]:
 539 |             raise ValueError(f"Missing {key!r} for MODEL_TENSORS of {self.model_arch!r}")
 540 |         name: str = gguf.TENSOR_NAMES[key]
 541 |         if "{bid}" in name:
 542 |             assert bid is not None
 543 |             name = name.format(bid=bid)
 544 |         return name + suffix
 545 | 
 546 |     def match_model_tensor_name(self, name: str, key: gguf.MODEL_TENSOR, bid: int | None, suffix: str = ".weight") -> bool:
 547 |         if key not in gguf.MODEL_TENSORS[self.model_arch]:
 548 |             return False
 549 |         key_name: str = gguf.TENSOR_NAMES[key]
 550 |         if "{bid}" in key_name:
 551 |             if bid is None:
 552 |                 return False
 553 |             key_name = key_name.format(bid=bid)
 554 |         else:
 555 |             if bid is not None:
 556 |                 return False
 557 |         return name == (key_name + suffix)
 558 | 
 559 |     def map_tensor_name(self, name: str, try_suffixes: Sequence[str] = (".weight", ".bias")) -> str:
 560 |         new_name = self.tensor_map.get_name(key=name, try_suffixes=try_suffixes)
 561 |         if new_name is None:
 562 |             raise ValueError(f"Can not map tensor {name!r}")
 563 |         return new_name
 564 | 
 565 |     def set_gguf_parameters(self):
 566 |         raise NotImplementedError("set_gguf_parameters() must be implemented in subclasses")
 567 | 
 568 |     def modify_tensors(self, data_torch: Tensor, name: str, bid: int | None) -> Iterable[tuple[str, Tensor]]:
 569 |         new_name = self.map_tensor_name(name)
 570 | 
 571 |         # Handle gate/up expert tensor fusion if enabled
 572 |         if self.fuse_gate_up_exps and bid is not None:
 573 |             if self.match_model_tensor_name(new_name, gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.FFN_GATE_EXP, bid):
 574 |                 self._gate_exp_buffer[bid] = data_torch
 575 |             elif self.match_model_tensor_name(new_name, gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.FFN_UP_EXP, bid):
 576 |                 self._up_exp_buffer[bid] = data_torch
 577 | 
 578 |             # Check if both gate and up are buffered for this layer
 579 |             if bid in self._gate_exp_buffer and bid in self._up_exp_buffer:
 580 |                 gate_data = self._gate_exp_buffer.pop(bid)
 581 |                 up_data = self._up_exp_buffer.pop(bid)
 582 |                 # gate/up shape: (n_expert, n_ff, n_embd), concatenate to (n_expert, n_ff*2, n_embd)
 583 |                 fused_data = torch.cat([gate_data, up_data], dim=1)
 584 |                 fused_name = self.format_tensor_name(gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.FFN_GATE_UP_EXP, bid)
 585 |                 logger.info(f"Fused gate_exps and up_exps for layer {bid}")
 586 |                 return [(fused_name, fused_data)]
 587 | 
 588 |             # If we buffered a gate/up tensor, wait for the other
 589 |             if self.match_model_tensor_name(new_name, gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.FFN_GATE_EXP, bid) or \
 590 |                self.match_model_tensor_name(new_name, gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.FFN_UP_EXP, bid):
 591 |                 return []
 592 | 
 593 |         return [(new_name, data_torch)]
 594 | 
 595 |     def tensor_force_quant(self, name: str, new_name: str, bid: int | None, n_dims: int) -> gguf.GGMLQuantizationType | bool:
 596 |         del name, new_name, bid, n_dims  # unused
 597 | 
 598 |         return False
 599 | 
 600 |     # some models need extra generated tensors (like rope_freqs)
 601 |     def generate_extra_tensors(self) -> Iterable[tuple[str, Tensor]]:
 602 |         return ()
 603 | 
 604 |     @staticmethod
 605 |     def _nvfp4_pack(weight: Tensor, scale: Tensor) -> tuple[np.ndarray, list[int]]:
 606 |         """Repack NVFP4 ModelOpt tensors into ggml super-block layout.
 607 |         Preserves original E4M3 scale bits as UE4M3 (strip sign bit).
 608 |         The per-tensor scale2 factor is stored as a separate tensor and applied at inference time via ggml_mul().
 609 |         Returns (raw_data, logical_shape)."""
 610 | 
 611 |         out_features = weight.shape[0]
 612 |         n_blocks = scale.shape[1]
 613 | 
 614 |         # Unpack ModelOpt nibble-packed weights
 615 |         w = weight.reshape(out_features, n_blocks, 8)
 616 |         vals = torch.stack([w & 0x0F, w >> 4], dim=-1).reshape(out_features, n_blocks, 16)
 617 | 
 618 |         # Preserve original E4M3 scale bits as UE4M3 (strip sign bit)
 619 |         d_ue = scale.view(torch.uint8).numpy().reshape(out_features, n_blocks) & 0x7F
 620 |         qs = (vals[:, :, :8] | (vals[:, :, 8:] << 4)).to(torch.uint8).numpy()
 621 | 
 622 |         # Pack into super-blocks: [4 UE4M3 scales, 32 qs bytes] = 36 bytes per 64 elements
 623 |         n_super = n_blocks // 4
 624 |         d_grouped = d_ue.reshape(out_features, n_super, 4)
 625 |         qs_grouped = qs.reshape(out_features, n_super, 4, 8).reshape(out_features, n_super, 32)
 626 |         raw = np.concatenate([d_grouped, qs_grouped], axis=-1).reshape(out_features, n_super * 36)
 627 |         return raw, [out_features, n_super * 64]
 628 | 
 629 |     def _repack_nvfp4(self, name: str, weight: Tensor, scale: Tensor, scale2: Tensor, input_scale: Tensor):
 630 |         if "language_model." in name:
 631 |             name = name.replace("language_model.", "")
 632 | 
 633 |         new_name = self.map_tensor_name(name)
 634 | 
 635 |         raw, shape = self._nvfp4_pack(weight, scale)
 636 |         logger.info(f"Repacked {new_name} with shape {shape} and quantization NVFP4")
 637 |         self.gguf_writer.add_tensor(new_name, raw, raw_dtype=gguf.GGMLQuantizationType.NVFP4)
 638 | 
 639 |         self._write_scale_tensor(new_name.replace(".weight", ".scale"), scale2)
 640 |         self._write_scale_tensor(new_name.replace(".weight", ".input_scale"), input_scale)
 641 | 
 642 |     def _generate_nvfp4_tensors(self):
 643 |         # Per-layer expert merging to avoid holding all experts in memory
 644 |         expert_blocks: dict[tuple[int, str], list[tuple[int, np.ndarray]]] = {}
 645 |         expert_scales: dict[tuple[int, str], list[tuple[int, float]]] = {}
 646 |         expert_input_scales: dict[tuple[int, str], list[tuple[int, float]]] = {}
 647 |         expert_shapes: dict[tuple[int, str], list[int]] = {}
 648 |         n_experts = self.find_hparam(["num_local_experts", "num_experts"], optional=True) or 0
 649 |         consumed: list[str] = []
 650 | 
 651 |         for name in list(self.model_tensors.keys()):
 652 |             if not name.endswith(".weight"):
 653 |                 continue
 654 |             scale_name = name.replace(".weight", ".weight_scale")
 655 |             scale2_name = name.replace(".weight", ".weight_scale_2")
 656 |             input_scale_name = name.replace(".weight", ".input_scale")
 657 |             if scale_name not in self.model_tensors:
 658 |                 continue
 659 |             # Force eager materialization of lazy tensors
 660 |             weight = LazyTorchTensor.to_eager(self.model_tensors[name]())
 661 |             scale = LazyTorchTensor.to_eager(self.model_tensors[scale_name]())
 662 | 
 663 |             # Skip non-NVFP4 tensors (e.g. FP8 with per-channel 1D scales)
 664 |             if scale.ndim < 2:
 665 |                 continue
 666 | 
 667 |             scale2 = LazyTorchTensor.to_eager(self.model_tensors.get(scale2_name, lambda: torch.tensor(1.0))())
 668 |             input_scale = LazyTorchTensor.to_eager(self.model_tensors.get(input_scale_name, lambda: torch.tensor(1.0))())
 669 | 
 670 |             # Mark tensors for removal from model_tensors (already written to gguf)
 671 |             consumed.extend([name, scale_name])
 672 |             if scale2_name in self.model_tensors:
 673 |                 consumed.append(scale2_name)
 674 |             if input_scale_name in self.model_tensors:
 675 |                 consumed.append(input_scale_name)
 676 | 
 677 |             # Check if this is a per-expert tensor
 678 |             m = re.search(r'\.experts\.(\d+)\.(gate_proj|up_proj|down_proj)\.weight$', name)
 679 |             if m:
 680 |                 expert_id = int(m.group(1))
 681 |                 proj_type = m.group(2)
 682 |                 bid_m = re.search(r'\.layers\.(\d+)\.', name)
 683 |                 bid = int(bid_m.group(1)) if bid_m else 0
 684 |                 key = (bid, proj_type)
 685 | 
 686 |                 raw, shape = self._nvfp4_pack(weight, scale)
 687 | 
 688 |                 if key not in expert_blocks:
 689 |                     expert_blocks[key] = []
 690 |                     expert_scales[key] = []
 691 |                     expert_input_scales[key] = []
 692 |                     expert_shapes[key] = shape
 693 |                 expert_blocks[key].append((expert_id, raw.copy()))
 694 |                 # Collect per-expert scale2 (scalar per expert)
 695 |                 expert_scales[key].append((expert_id, float(scale2.float().sum())))
 696 |                 # Collect per-expert input_scale (scalar per expert)
 697 |                 expert_input_scales[key].append((expert_id, float(input_scale.float().sum())))
 698 | 
 699 |                 # Flush when all experts for this (layer, proj) are collected
 700 |                 if n_experts > 0 and len(expert_blocks[key]) >= n_experts:
 701 |                     self._flush_nvfp4_experts(key, expert_blocks, expert_scales, expert_input_scales, expert_shapes, bid, proj_type)
 702 |             else:
 703 |                 self._repack_nvfp4(name, weight, scale, scale2, input_scale)
 704 | 
 705 |         # Flush any remaining experts (fallback if n_experts was unknown)
 706 |         for (bid, proj_type) in list(expert_blocks.keys()):
 707 |             self._flush_nvfp4_experts((bid, proj_type), expert_blocks, expert_scales, expert_input_scales, expert_shapes, bid, proj_type)
 708 | 
 709 |         # Remove consumed tensors so get_tensors/modify_tensors won't see them
 710 |         for name in consumed:
 711 |             self.model_tensors.pop(name, None)
 712 | 
 713 |         # Remove any remaining unused auxiliary tensors
 714 |         for name in list(self.model_tensors.keys()):
 715 |             if name.endswith((".k_scale", ".v_scale")):
 716 |                 del self.model_tensors[name]
 717 | 
 718 |     def _flush_nvfp4_experts(self, key, expert_blocks, expert_scales, expert_input_scales, expert_shapes, bid, proj_type):
 719 |         experts = expert_blocks.pop(key)
 720 |         scales = expert_scales.pop(key)
 721 |         input_scales = expert_input_scales.pop(key)
 722 |         shape = expert_shapes.pop(key)
 723 | 
 724 |         experts.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
 725 |         merged = np.stack([e[1] for e in experts], axis=0)
 726 |         merged_name = f"model.layers.{bid}.mlp.experts.{proj_type}.weight"
 727 |         new_name = self.map_tensor_name(merged_name)
 728 |         logger.info(f"Repacked {new_name} with shape [{len(experts)}, {shape[0]}, {shape[1]}] and quantization NVFP4")
 729 |         self.gguf_writer.add_tensor(new_name, merged, raw_dtype=gguf.GGMLQuantizationType.NVFP4)
 730 | 
 731 |         scales.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
 732 |         self._write_scales_tensor(new_name.replace(".weight", ".scale"), [s[1] for s in scales])
 733 | 
 734 |         input_scales.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
 735 |         self._write_scales_tensor(new_name.replace(".weight", ".input_scale"), [s[1] for s in input_scales])
 736 | 
 737 |         del experts, merged
 738 | 
 739 |     def _needs_nvfp4_processing(self) -> bool:
 740 |         return True
 741 | 
 742 |     @staticmethod
 743 |     def _tensor_sort_key(name: str) -> tuple[int, int, str]:
 744 |         parts = name.split(".")
 745 |         if name == "rope_freqs" or name.endswith(".rotary_emb.inv_freq"):
 746 |             return (0, 0, name)
 747 |         if any(part in {"embed_tokens", "tok_embeddings", "word_embeddings", "wte"} for part in parts):
 748 |             return (0, 1, name)
 749 | 
 750 |         for part in parts:
 751 |             if part.isdecimal():
 752 |                 return (1, int(part), name)
 753 | 
 754 |         if parts[-1] in {"norm", "ln_f"} or name.endswith((".norm.weight", ".ln_f.weight")):
 755 |             return (2, 0, name)
 756 |         if parts[0] in {"lm_head", "output"}:
 757 |             return (3, 0, name)
 758 |         return (4, 0, name)
 759 | 
 760 |     def prepare_tensors(self):
 761 |         # detect NVFP4 quantization (ModelOpt format)
 762 |         quant_algo = (self.hparams.get("quantization_config") or {}).get("quant_algo")
 763 |         quant_method = (self.hparams.get("quantization_config") or {}).get("quant_method")
 764 |         quant_layers = (self.hparams.get("quantization_config") or {}).get("quantized_layers") or {}
 765 |         quant_config_file = self.dir_model / "hf_quant_config.json"
 766 | 
 767 |         if (not quant_algo or not quant_layers) and quant_config_file.is_file():
 768 |             with open(quant_config_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
 769 |                 hf_quant_config = json.load(f)
 770 |                 quant_config = hf_quant_config.get("quantization") or {}
 771 |                 producer = hf_quant_config.get("producer") or {}
 772 |                 producer_name = (producer.get("name") or "").lower()
 773 |                 if quant_method is None:
 774 |                     self.hparams.setdefault("quantization_config", {})["quant_method"] = producer_name
 775 |                 quant_algo = quant_config.get("quant_algo", quant_algo)
 776 |                 quant_layers = quant_config.get("quantized_layers", quant_layers) or {}
 777 | 
 778 |         # Some models use per-tensor quant_algo (e.g. "MIXED_PRECISION" with
 779 |         # per-layer NVFP4/FP8) instead of a single global "NVFP4" value.
 780 |         if quant_algo != "NVFP4":
 781 |             if any(v.get("quant_algo") == "NVFP4" for v in quant_layers.values() if isinstance(v, dict)):
 782 |                 quant_algo = "NVFP4"
 783 | 
 784 |         self._is_nvfp4 = quant_algo == "NVFP4"
 785 |         self._is_mxfp4 = quant_method == "mxfp4"
 786 | 
 787 |         # NVFP4 weights are repacked and written directly to gguf_writer.
 788 |         # This must run before dequant_model so NVFP4 tensors are removed
 789 |         # from model_tensors, leaving only non-NVFP4 (e.g. FP8) for dequant.
 790 |         if self._is_nvfp4 and self._needs_nvfp4_processing():
 791 |             self._generate_nvfp4_tensors()
 792 | 
 793 |         self.dequant_model()
 794 | 
 795 |         # Handle empty tensor_map for models with block_count=0 (like MobileNetV5)
 796 |         if self.tensor_map.mapping:
 797 |             max_name_len = max(len(s) for _, s in self.tensor_map.mapping.values()) + len(".weight,")
 798 |         else:
 799 |             max_name_len = len("vision_encoder.weight,")  # Default reasonable length
 800 | 
 801 |         all_tensors = list(chain(self.generate_extra_tensors(), self.get_tensors()))
 802 |         if self.sort_tensors:
 803 |             all_tensors.sort(key=lambda item: self._tensor_sort_key(item[0]))
 804 | 
 805 |         for name, data_torch in all_tensors:
 806 |             # we don't need these
 807 |             if name.endswith((".attention.masked_bias", ".attention.bias", ".rotary_emb.inv_freq")):
 808 |                 continue
 809 | 
 810 |             old_dtype = data_torch.dtype
 811 | 
 812 |             # convert any unsupported data types to float32
 813 |             if data_torch.dtype not in (torch.float16, torch.float32):
 814 |                 data_torch = data_torch.to(torch.float32)
 815 | 
 816 |             # use the first number-like part of the tensor name as the block id
 817 |             bid = None
 818 |             for part in name.split("."):
 819 |                 if part.isdecimal():
 820 |                     bid = int(part)
 821 |                     break
 822 | 
 823 |             for new_name, data_torch in (self.modify_tensors(data_torch, name, bid)):
 824 |                 # TODO: why do we squeeze here?
 825 |                 # data = data_torch.squeeze().numpy()
 826 |                 data = data_torch.numpy()
 827 | 
 828 |                 n_dims = len(data.shape)
 829 |                 data_qtype: gguf.GGMLQuantizationType | bool = self.tensor_force_quant(name, new_name, bid, n_dims)
 830 | 
 831 |                 # Most of the codebase that takes in 1D tensors or norms only handles F32 tensors
 832 |                 if n_dims <= 1 or new_name.endswith("_norm.weight"):
 833 |                     data_qtype = gguf.GGMLQuantizationType.F32
 834 | 
 835 |                 # Conditions should closely match those in llama_model_quantize_internal in llama.cpp
 836 |                 # Some tensor types are always in float32
 837 |                 if data_qtype is False and (
 838 |                     any(
 839 |                         self.match_model_tensor_name(new_name, key, bid)
 840 |                         for key in (
 841 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.FFN_GATE_INP,
 842 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.FFN_GATE_INP_SHEXP,
 843 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.POS_EMBD,
 844 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.TOKEN_TYPES,
 845 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.SSM_CONV1D,
 846 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.SHORTCONV_CONV,
 847 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.TIME_MIX_FIRST,
 848 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.TIME_MIX_W1,
 849 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.TIME_MIX_W2,
 850 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.TIME_MIX_DECAY_W1,
 851 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.TIME_MIX_DECAY_W2,
 852 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.TIME_MIX_LERP_FUSED,
 853 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.POSNET_NORM1,
 854 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.POSNET_NORM2,
 855 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.V_ENC_EMBD_POS,
 856 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.A_ENC_EMBD_POS,
 857 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.ALTUP_CORRECT_COEF,
 858 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.ALTUP_PREDICT_COEF,
 859 |                             # Kimi KDA conv weights should be F32
 860 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.SSM_CONV1D_Q,
 861 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.SSM_CONV1D_K,
 862 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.SSM_CONV1D_V,
 863 |                         )
 864 |                     )
 865 |                     or new_name[-7:] not in (".weight", ".lora_a", ".lora_b")
 866 |                 ):
 867 |                     data_qtype = gguf.GGMLQuantizationType.F32
 868 | 
 869 |                 if data_qtype is False and any(
 870 |                     self.match_model_tensor_name(new_name, key, bid)
 871 |                     for key in (
 872 |                         gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.TOKEN_EMBD,
 873 |                         gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.PER_LAYER_TOKEN_EMBD,
 874 |                         gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.OUTPUT,
 875 |                         gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.ALTUP_ROUTER,
 876 |                         gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.LAUREL_L,
 877 |                         gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.LAUREL_R,
 878 |                     )
 879 |                 ):
 880 |                     if self.ftype in (
 881 |                         gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_TQ1_0,
 882 |                         gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_TQ2_0,
 883 |                     ):
 884 |                         # TODO: use Q4_K and Q6_K
 885 |                         data_qtype = gguf.GGMLQuantizationType.F16
 886 | 
 887 |                 # No override (data_qtype is False), or wants to be quantized (data_qtype is True)
 888 |                 if isinstance(data_qtype, bool):
 889 |                     if self.ftype == gguf.LlamaFileType.ALL_F32:
 890 |                         data_qtype = gguf.GGMLQuantizationType.F32
 891 |                     elif self.ftype == gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_F16:
 892 |                         data_qtype = gguf.GGMLQuantizationType.F16
 893 |                     elif self.ftype == gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_BF16:
 894 |                         data_qtype = gguf.GGMLQuantizationType.BF16
 895 |                     elif self.ftype == gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_Q8_0:
 896 |                         data_qtype = gguf.GGMLQuantizationType.Q8_0
 897 |                     elif self.ftype == gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_TQ1_0:
 898 |                         data_qtype = gguf.GGMLQuantizationType.TQ1_0
 899 |                     elif self.ftype == gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_TQ2_0:
 900 |                         data_qtype = gguf.GGMLQuantizationType.TQ2_0
 901 |                     else:
 902 |                         raise ValueError(f"Unknown file type: {self.ftype.name}")
 903 | 
 904 |                 try:
 905 |                     data = gguf.quants.quantize(data, data_qtype)
 906 |                 except gguf.QuantError as e:
 907 |                     logger.warning("%s, %s", e, "falling back to F16")
 908 |                     data_qtype = gguf.GGMLQuantizationType.F16
 909 |                     data = gguf.quants.quantize(data, data_qtype)
 910 | 
 911 |                 shape = gguf.quant_shape_from_byte_shape(data.shape, data_qtype) if data.dtype == np.uint8 else data.shape
 912 | 
 913 |                 # reverse shape to make it similar to the internal ggml dimension order
 914 |                 shape_str = f"{{{', '.join(str(n) for n in reversed(shape))}}}"
 915 | 
 916 |                 # n_dims is implicit in the shape
 917 |                 logger.info(f"{f'%-{max_name_len}s' % f'{new_name},'} {old_dtype} --> {data_qtype.name}, shape = {shape_str}")
 918 | 
 919 |                 self.gguf_writer.add_tensor(new_name, data, raw_dtype=data_qtype)
 920 | 
 921 |     def set_type(self):
 922 |         self.gguf_writer.add_type(gguf.GGUFType.MODEL)
 923 | 
 924 |     def prepare_metadata(self, vocab_only: bool):
 925 | 
 926 |         total_params, shared_params, expert_params, expert_count = self.gguf_writer.get_total_parameter_count()
 927 | 
 928 |         self.metadata = gguf.Metadata.load(self.metadata_override, self.dir_model_card, self.model_name, total_params)
 929 | 
 930 |         # If we are using HF model id, set the metadata name to the model id
 931 |         if self.remote_hf_model_id:
 932 |             self.metadata.name = self.remote_hf_model_id
 933 | 
 934 |         # Fallback to model directory name if metadata name is still missing
 935 |         if self.metadata.name is None:
 936 |             self.metadata.name = self.dir_model.name
 937 | 
 938 |         if self.ftype in (gguf.LlamaFileType.ALL_F32, gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_F16, gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_BF16):
 939 |             if self._is_nvfp4:
 940 |                 self.ftype = gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_NVFP4
 941 |             elif self._is_mxfp4:
 942 |                 self.ftype = gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_MXFP4_MOE
 943 | 
 944 |         # Generate parameter weight class (useful for leader boards) if not yet determined
 945 |         if self.metadata.size_label is None and total_params > 0:
 946 |             self.metadata.size_label = gguf.size_label(total_params, shared_params, expert_params, expert_count)
 947 | 
 948 |         self.set_type()
 949 | 
 950 |         logger.info("Set meta model")
 951 |         self.metadata.set_gguf_meta_model(self.gguf_writer)
 952 | 
 953 |         logger.info("Set model parameters")
 954 |         self.set_gguf_parameters()
 955 | 
 956 |         logger.info("Set model quantization version")
 957 |         self.gguf_writer.add_quantization_version(gguf.GGML_QUANT_VERSION)
 958 | 
 959 |     def write_vocab(self):
 960 |         raise NotImplementedError("write_vocab() must be implemented in subclasses")
 961 | 
 962 |     def write(self):
 963 |         self.prepare_tensors()
 964 |         self.prepare_metadata(vocab_only=False)
 965 |         self.gguf_writer.write_header_to_file(path=self.fname_out)
 966 |         self.gguf_writer.write_kv_data_to_file()
 967 |         self.gguf_writer.write_tensors_to_file(progress=True)
 968 |         self.gguf_writer.close()
 969 | 
 970 |     @staticmethod
 971 |     def get_model_part_names(dir_model: Path, prefix: str, suffix: str) -> list[str]:
 972 |         part_names: list[str] = []
 973 |         for filename in os.listdir(dir_model):
 974 |             if filename.startswith(prefix) and filename.endswith(suffix):
 975 |                 part_names.append(filename)
 976 | 
 977 |         part_names.sort()
 978 | 
 979 |         return part_names
 980 | 
 981 |     @staticmethod
 982 |     def load_hparams(dir_model: Path, is_mistral_format: bool):
 983 |         if is_mistral_format:
 984 |             with open(dir_model / "params.json", "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
 985 |                 config = json.load(f)
 986 |             return config
 987 | 
 988 |         try:
 989 |             # for security reason, we don't allow loading remote code by default
 990 |             # if a model need remote code, we will fallback to config.json
 991 |             config = AutoConfig.from_pretrained(dir_model, trust_remote_code=False).to_dict()
 992 |         except Exception as e:
 993 |             logger.warning(f"Failed to load model config from {dir_model}: {e}")
 994 |             logger.warning("Trying to load config.json instead")
 995 |             with open(dir_model / "config.json", "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
 996 |                 config = json.load(f)
 997 |         if "llm_config" in config:
 998 |             # rename for InternVL
 999 |             config["text_config"] = config["llm_config"]
1000 |         if "lm_config" in config:
1001 |             # rename for GlmASR
1002 |             config["text_config"] = config["lm_config"]
1003 |         if "thinker_config" in config:
1004 |             # rename for Qwen2.5-Omni
1005 |             config["text_config"] = config["thinker_config"]["text_config"]
1006 |         if "language_config" in config:
1007 |             # rename for DeepSeekOCR
1008 |             config["text_config"] = config["language_config"]
1009 |         if "lfm" in config:
1010 |             # rename for LFM2-Audio
1011 |             config["text_config"] = config["lfm"]
1012 |         return config
1013 | 
1014 |     @classmethod
1015 |     def register(cls, *names: str) -> Callable[[AnyModel], AnyModel]:
1016 |         assert names
1017 | 
1018 |         def func(modelcls: AnyModel) -> AnyModel:
1019 |             model_type = ModelType.MMPROJ if modelcls.model_arch == gguf.MODEL_ARCH.MMPROJ else ModelType.TEXT
1020 |             for name in names:
1021 |                 cls._model_classes[model_type][name] = modelcls
1022 |             return modelcls
1023 |         return func
1024 | 
1025 |     @classmethod
1026 |     def print_registered_models(cls):
1027 |         for model_type, model_classes in cls._model_classes.items():
1028 |             logger.error(f"{model_type.name} models:")
1029 |             for name in sorted(model_classes.keys()):
1030 |                 logger.error(f"  - {name}")
1031 | 
1032 |     @classmethod
1033 |     def from_model_architecture(cls, arch: str, model_type = ModelType.TEXT) -> type[ModelBase]:
1034 |         try:
1035 |             return cls._model_classes[model_type][arch]
1036 |         except KeyError:
1037 |             raise NotImplementedError(f'Architecture {arch!r} not supported!') from None
1038 | 
1039 | 
1040 | class TextModel(ModelBase):
1041 |     model_type = ModelType.TEXT
1042 |     hf_arch: str
1043 | 
1044 |     def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
1045 |         super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
1046 |         if not self.is_mistral_format:
1047 |             self.hf_arch = get_model_architecture(self.hparams, self.model_type)
1048 |         else:
1049 |             self.hf_arch = ""
1050 | 
1051 |         if "text_config" in self.hparams:
1052 |             # move the text_config to the root level
1053 |             self.hparams = {**self.hparams, **self.hparams["text_config"]}
1054 | 
1055 |         self.block_count = self.find_hparam(["n_layers", "num_hidden_layers", "n_layer", "num_layers"])
1056 |         self.tensor_map = gguf.get_tensor_name_map(self.model_arch, self.block_count)
1057 | 
1058 |         self.rope_parameters = self.hparams.get("rope_parameters", self.hparams.get("rope_scaling")) or {}
1059 | 
1060 |         rope_theta = self.find_hparam(["global_rope_theta", "rope_global_theta", "rope_theta_global", "rope_theta", "rotary_emb_base"], optional=True)
1061 |         local_rope_theta = self.find_hparam(["local_rope_theta", "rope_local_theta", "rope_theta_local", "swa_rope_theta", "rope_local_base_freq"], optional=True)
1062 | 
1063 |         # Ensure "rope_theta" and "rope_type" is mirrored in rope_parameters
1064 |         if "full_attention" not in self.rope_parameters and "sliding_attention" not in self.rope_parameters:
1065 |             if local_rope_theta is not None:
1066 |                 self.rope_parameters["sliding_attention"] = {"rope_theta": local_rope_theta}
1067 |             if "rope_theta" not in self.rope_parameters and rope_theta is not None:
1068 |                 self.rope_parameters["rope_theta"] = rope_theta
1069 |             if "rope_type" not in self.rope_parameters and (rope_type := self.rope_parameters.get("type")) is not None:
1070 |                 self.rope_parameters["rope_type"] = rope_type
1071 | 
1072 |     @classmethod
1073 |     def __init_subclass__(cls):
1074 |         # can't use an abstract property, because overriding it without type errors
1075 |         # would require using decorated functions instead of simply defining the property
1076 |         if "model_arch" not in cls.__dict__:
1077 |             raise TypeError(f"Missing property 'model_arch' for {cls.__name__!r}")
1078 | 
1079 |     def set_vocab(self):
1080 |         self._set_vocab_gpt2()
1081 | 
1082 |     def prepare_metadata(self, vocab_only: bool):
1083 |         super().prepare_metadata(vocab_only=vocab_only)
1084 | 
1085 |         total_params = self.gguf_writer.get_total_parameter_count()[0]
1086 |         # Extract the encoding scheme from the file type name. e.g. 'gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_Q8_0' --> 'Q8_0'
1087 |         output_type: str = self.ftype.name.partition("_")[2]
1088 | 
1089 |         # Filename Output
1090 |         if self.fname_out.is_dir():
1091 |             # Generate default filename based on model specification and available metadata
1092 |             if not vocab_only:
1093 |                 fname_default: str = gguf.naming_convention(self.metadata.name, self.metadata.basename, self.metadata.finetune, self.metadata.version, self.metadata.size_label, output_type, model_type="LoRA" if total_params < 0 else None)
1094 |             else:
1095 |                 fname_default: str = gguf.naming_convention(self.metadata.name, self.metadata.basename, self.metadata.finetune, self.metadata.version, size_label=None, output_type=None, model_type="vocab")
1096 | 
1097 |             # Use the default filename
1098 |             self.fname_out = self.fname_out / f"{fname_default}.gguf"
1099 |         else:
1100 |             # Output path is a custom defined templated filename
1101 |             # Note: `not is_dir()` is used because `.is_file()` will not detect
1102 |             #       file template strings as it doesn't actually exist as a file
1103 | 
1104 |             # Process templated file name with the output ftype, useful with the "auto" ftype
1105 |             self.fname_out = self.fname_out.parent / gguf.fill_templated_filename(self.fname_out.name, output_type)
1106 | 
1107 |         logger.info("Set model tokenizer")
1108 |         self.set_vocab()
1109 | 
1110 |     def set_gguf_parameters(self):
1111 |         self.gguf_writer.add_block_count(self.block_count)
1112 | 
1113 |         if (n_ctx := self.find_hparam(["max_position_embeddings", "n_ctx", "n_positions", "max_length", "max_sequence_length", "model_max_length"], optional=True)) is not None:
1114 |             self.gguf_writer.add_context_length(n_ctx)
1115 |             logger.info(f"gguf: context length = {n_ctx}")
1116 | 
1117 |         if (n_embd := self.find_hparam(["hidden_size", "n_embd", "dim"], optional=True)) is not None:
1118 |             self.gguf_writer.add_embedding_length(n_embd)
1119 |             logger.info(f"gguf: embedding length = {n_embd}")
1120 | 
1121 |         if (n_ff := self.find_hparam(["intermediate_size", "n_inner", "hidden_dim"], optional=True)) is not None:
1122 |             self.gguf_writer.add_feed_forward_length(n_ff)
1123 |             logger.info(f"gguf: feed forward length = {n_ff}")
1124 | 
1125 |         if (n_head := self.find_hparam(["num_attention_heads", "n_head", "n_heads"], optional=True)) is not None:
1126 |             self.gguf_writer.add_head_count(n_head)
1127 |             logger.info(f"gguf: head count = {n_head}")
1128 | 
1129 |         if (n_head_kv := self.find_hparam(["num_key_value_heads", "n_kv_heads"], optional=True)) is not None:
1130 |             self.gguf_writer.add_head_count_kv(n_head_kv)
1131 |             logger.info(f"gguf: key-value head count = {n_head_kv}")
1132 | 
1133 |         if self.hparams.get("is_causal") is False:
1134 |             self.gguf_writer.add_causal_attention(False)
1135 |             logger.info("gguf: causal attention = False")
1136 | 
1137 |         # TODO: Handle "sliding_attention" similarly when models start implementing it
1138 |         rope_params = self.rope_parameters.get("full_attention", self.rope_parameters)
1139 |         if (rope_type := rope_params.get("rope_type")) is not None:
1140 |             rope_factor = rope_params.get("factor")
1141 |             rope_gguf_type = gguf.RopeScalingType.NONE
1142 |             if rope_type == "linear" and rope_factor is not None:
1143 |                 rope_gguf_type = gguf.RopeScalingType.LINEAR
1144 |                 self.gguf_writer.add_rope_scaling_type(rope_gguf_type)
1145 |                 self.gguf_writer.add_rope_scaling_factor(rope_factor)
1146 |             elif rope_type == "yarn" and rope_factor is not None:
1147 |                 rope_gguf_type = gguf.RopeScalingType.YARN
1148 |                 self.gguf_writer.add_rope_scaling_type(rope_gguf_type)
1149 |                 self.gguf_writer.add_rope_scaling_factor(rope_factor)
1150 |                 self.gguf_writer.add_rope_scaling_orig_ctx_len(rope_params["original_max_position_embeddings"])
1151 |                 if (yarn_ext_factor := rope_params.get("extrapolation_factor")) is not None:
1152 |                     self.gguf_writer.add_rope_scaling_yarn_ext_factor(yarn_ext_factor)
1153 |                 if (yarn_attn_factor := rope_params.get("attention_factor", rope_params.get("attn_factor"))) is not None:
1154 |                     self.gguf_writer.add_rope_scaling_yarn_attn_factor(yarn_attn_factor)
1155 |                 if (yarn_beta_fast := rope_params.get("beta_fast")) is not None:
1156 |                     self.gguf_writer.add_rope_scaling_yarn_beta_fast(yarn_beta_fast)
1157 |                 if (yarn_beta_slow := rope_params.get("beta_slow")) is not None:
1158 |                     self.gguf_writer.add_rope_scaling_yarn_beta_slow(yarn_beta_slow)
1159 |                 # self.gguf_writer.add_rope_scaling_yarn_log_mul(rope_params["mscale_all_dim"])
1160 |             elif rope_type == "su" or rope_type == "longrope":
1161 |                 rope_gguf_type = gguf.RopeScalingType.LONGROPE
1162 |                 self.gguf_writer.add_rope_scaling_type(rope_gguf_type)
1163 |             elif rope_type == "dynamic":
1164 |                 # HunYuan, handled in model class
1165 |                 pass
1166 |             elif rope_type.lower() == "llama3":
1167 |                 # Handled in generate_extra_tensors
1168 |                 pass
1169 |             else:
1170 |                 logger.warning(f"Unknown RoPE type: {rope_type}")
1171 |             logger.info(f"gguf: rope scaling type = {rope_gguf_type.name}")
1172 | 
1173 |         if "mrope_section" in self.rope_parameters:
1174 |             mrope_section = self.rope_parameters["mrope_section"]
1175 |             # Pad to 4 dimensions [time, height, width, extra]
1176 |             while len(mrope_section) < 4:
1177 |                 mrope_section.append(0)
1178 |             self.gguf_writer.add_rope_dimension_sections(mrope_section[:4])
1179 |             logger.info(f"gguf: mrope sections: {mrope_section[:4]}")
1180 | 
1181 |         if (rope_theta := rope_params.get("rope_theta")) is not None:
1182 |             self.gguf_writer.add_rope_freq_base(rope_theta)
1183 |             logger.info(f"gguf: rope theta = {rope_theta}")
1184 |         if (local_rope_theta := self.rope_parameters.get("sliding_attention", {}).get("rope_theta")) is not None:
1185 |             self.gguf_writer.add_rope_freq_base_swa(local_rope_theta)
1186 |             logger.info(f"gguf: rope theta swa = {local_rope_theta}")
1187 |         if (f_rms_eps := self.find_hparam(["rms_norm_eps", "norm_eps"], optional=True)) is not None:
1188 |             self.gguf_writer.add_layer_norm_rms_eps(f_rms_eps)
1189 |             logger.info(f"gguf: rms norm epsilon = {f_rms_eps}")
1190 |         if (f_norm_eps := self.find_hparam(["layer_norm_eps", "layer_norm_epsilon", "norm_epsilon"], optional=True)) is not None:
1191 |             self.gguf_writer.add_layer_norm_eps(f_norm_eps)
1192 |             logger.info(f"gguf: layer norm epsilon = {f_norm_eps}")
1193 |         if (n_experts := self.find_hparam(["num_local_experts", "num_experts"], optional=True)) is not None:
1194 |             self.gguf_writer.add_expert_count(n_experts)
1195 |             logger.info(f"gguf: expert count = {n_experts}")
1196 |         if (n_experts_used := self.find_hparam(["num_experts_per_tok", "num_experts_per_token", "top_k_experts"], optional=True)) is not None:
1197 |             self.gguf_writer.add_expert_used_count(n_experts_used)
1198 |             logger.info(f"gguf: experts used count = {n_experts_used}")
1199 |         if (n_expert_groups := self.hparams.get("n_group")) is not None:
1200 |             self.gguf_writer.add_expert_group_count(n_expert_groups)
1201 |             logger.info(f"gguf: expert groups count = {n_expert_groups}")
1202 |         if (n_group_used := self.hparams.get("topk_group")) is not None:
1203 |             self.gguf_writer.add_expert_group_used_count(n_group_used)
1204 |             logger.info(f"gguf: expert groups used count = {n_group_used}")
1205 | 
1206 |         if (score_func := self.find_hparam(["score_function", "scoring_func", "score_func", "moe_router_activation", "moe_router_activation_func"], optional=True)) is not None:
1207 |             if score_func == "sigmoid":
1208 |                 self.gguf_writer.add_expert_gating_func(gguf.ExpertGatingFuncType.SIGMOID)
1209 |             elif score_func == "softmax":
1210 |                 self.gguf_writer.add_expert_gating_func(gguf.ExpertGatingFuncType.SOFTMAX)
1211 |             else:
1212 |                 raise ValueError(f"Unsupported expert score gating function value: {score_func}")
1213 |             logger.info(f"gguf: expert score gating function = {score_func}")
1214 | 
1215 |         if (head_dim := self.hparams.get("head_dim")) is not None:
1216 |             self.gguf_writer.add_key_length(head_dim)
1217 |             self.gguf_writer.add_value_length(head_dim)
1218 | 
1219 |         self.gguf_writer.add_file_type(self.ftype)
1220 |         logger.info(f"gguf: file type = {self.ftype}")
1221 | 
1222 |     def write_vocab(self):
1223 |         if len(self.gguf_writer.tensors) != 1:
1224 |             raise ValueError('Splitting the vocabulary is not supported')
1225 | 
1226 |         self.prepare_metadata(vocab_only=True)
1227 |         self.gguf_writer.write_header_to_file(path=self.fname_out)
1228 |         self.gguf_writer.write_kv_data_to_file()
1229 |         self.gguf_writer.close()
1230 | 
1231 |     def does_token_look_special(self, token: str | bytes) -> bool:
1232 |         if isinstance(token, (bytes, bytearray)):
1233 |             token_text = token.decode(encoding="utf-8")
1234 |         elif isinstance(token, memoryview):
1235 |             token_text = token.tobytes().decode(encoding="utf-8")
1236 |         else:
1237 |             token_text = token
1238 | 
1239 |         # Some models mark some added tokens which ought to be control tokens as not special.
1240 |         # (e.g. command-r, command-r-plus, deepseek-coder, gemma{,-2})
1241 |         seems_special = token_text in (
1242 |             "<pad>",  # deepseek-coder
1243 |             "<mask>", "<2mass>", "[@BOS@]",  # gemma{,-2}
1244 |         )
1245 | 
1246 |         seems_special = seems_special or (token_text.startswith("<|") and token_text.endswith("|>"))
1247 |         seems_special = seems_special or (token_text.startswith("<｜") and token_text.endswith("｜>"))  # deepseek-coder
1248 | 
1249 |         # TODO: should these be marked as UNUSED instead? (maybe not)
1250 |         seems_special = seems_special or (token_text.startswith("<unused") and token_text.endswith(">"))  # gemma{,-2}
1251 | 
1252 |         return seems_special
1253 | 
1254 |     # used for GPT-2 BPE and WordPiece vocabs
1255 |     def get_vocab_base(self) -> tuple[list[str], list[int], str]:
1256 |         tokens: list[str] = []
1257 |         toktypes: list[int] = []
1258 | 
1259 |         from transformers import AutoTokenizer
1260 |         tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(self.dir_model)
1261 |         vocab_size = self.hparams.get("vocab_size", len(tokenizer.vocab))  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]
1262 |         assert max(tokenizer.vocab.values()) < vocab_size  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]
1263 | 
1264 |         tokpre = self.get_vocab_base_pre(tokenizer)
1265 | 
1266 |         reverse_vocab = {id_: encoded_tok for encoded_tok, id_ in tokenizer.vocab.items()}  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]
1267 |         added_vocab = tokenizer.get_added_vocab()  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]
1268 | 
1269 |         added_tokens_decoder = tokenizer.added_tokens_decoder  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]
1270 | 
1271 |         for i in range(vocab_size):
1272 |             if i not in reverse_vocab:
1273 |                 tokens.append(f"[PAD{i}]")
1274 |                 toktypes.append(gguf.TokenType.UNUSED)
1275 |             else:
1276 |                 token: str = reverse_vocab[i]
1277 |                 if token in added_vocab:
1278 |                     # The tokenizer in llama.cpp assumes the CONTROL and USER_DEFINED tokens are pre-normalized.
1279 |                     # To avoid unexpected issues - we make sure to normalize non-normalized tokens
1280 |                     if not added_tokens_decoder[i].normalized:
1281 |                         previous_token = token
1282 |                         token = tokenizer.decode(tokenizer.encode(token, add_special_tokens=False))  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute, invalid-assignment]
1283 |                         if previous_token != token:
1284 |                             logger.info(f"{repr(previous_token)} is encoded and decoded back to {repr(token)} using AutoTokenizer")
1285 | 
1286 |                     if added_tokens_decoder[i].special or self.does_token_look_special(token):
1287 |                         toktypes.append(gguf.TokenType.CONTROL)
1288 |                     else:
1289 |                         # NOTE: this was added for Gemma.
1290 |                         # Encoding and decoding the tokens above isn't sufficient for this case.
1291 |                         token = token.replace(b"\xe2\x96\x81".decode("utf-8"), " ")  # pre-normalize user-defined spaces
1292 |                         toktypes.append(gguf.TokenType.USER_DEFINED)
1293 |                 else:
1294 |                     toktypes.append(gguf.TokenType.NORMAL)
1295 |                 tokens.append(token)
1296 | 
1297 |         return tokens, toktypes, tokpre
1298 | 
1299 |     # NOTE: this function is generated by convert_hf_to_gguf_update.py
1300 |     #       do not modify it manually!
1301 |     # ref:  https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/pull/6920
1302 |     # Marker: Start get_vocab_base_pre
1303 |     def get_vocab_base_pre(self, tokenizer) -> str:
1304 |         # encoding this string and hashing the resulting tokens would (hopefully) give us a unique identifier that
1305 |         # is specific for the BPE pre-tokenizer used by the model
1306 |         # we will use this unique identifier to write a "tokenizer.ggml.pre" entry in the GGUF file which we can
1307 |         # use in llama.cpp to implement the same pre-tokenizer
1308 | 
1309 |         chktxt = '\n \n\n \n\n\n \t \t\t \t\n  \n   \n    \n     \n🚀 (normal) 😶\u200d🌫️ (multiple emojis concatenated) ✅ 🦙🦙 3 33 333 3333 33333 333333 3333333 33333333 3.3 3..3 3...3 កាន់តែពិសេសអាច😁 ?我想在apple工作1314151天～ ------======= нещо на Български \'\'\'\'\'\'```````""""......!!!!!!?????? I\'ve been \'told he\'s there, \'RE you sure? \'M not sure I\'ll make it, \'D you like some tea? We\'Ve a\'lL'
1310 | 
1311 |         chktok = tokenizer.encode(chktxt)
1312 |         chkhsh = sha256(str(chktok).encode()).hexdigest()
1313 | 
1314 |         logger.debug(f"chktok: {chktok}")
1315 |         logger.debug(f"chkhsh: {chkhsh}")
1316 | 
1317 |         res = None
1318 | 
1319 |         # NOTE: if you get an error here, you need to update the convert_hf_to_gguf_update.py script
1320 |         #       or pull the latest version of the model from Huggingface
1321 |         #       don't edit the hashes manually!
1322 |         if chkhsh == "b6e8e1518dc4305be2fe39c313ed643381c4da5db34a98f6a04c093f8afbe99b":
