1580 |             logger.warning("** ref:     https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/pull/6920")
1581 |             logger.warning("**")
1582 |             logger.warning(f"** chkhsh:  {chkhsh}")
1583 |             logger.warning("**************************************************************************************")
1584 |             logger.warning("\n")
1585 |             raise NotImplementedError("BPE pre-tokenizer was not recognized - update get_vocab_base_pre()")
1586 | 
1587 |         logger.debug(f"tokenizer.ggml.pre: {repr(res)}")
1588 |         logger.debug(f"chkhsh: {chkhsh}")
1589 | 
1590 |         return res
1591 |         # Marker: End get_vocab_base_pre
1592 | 
1593 |     def _set_vocab_none(self) -> None:
1594 |         self.gguf_writer.add_tokenizer_model("none")
1595 | 
1596 |     def _set_vocab_gpt2(self) -> None:
1597 |         tokens, toktypes, tokpre = self.get_vocab_base()
1598 |         self.gguf_writer.add_tokenizer_model("gpt2")
1599 |         self.gguf_writer.add_tokenizer_pre(tokpre)
1600 |         self.gguf_writer.add_token_list(tokens)
1601 |         self.gguf_writer.add_token_types(toktypes)
1602 | 
1603 |         special_vocab = gguf.SpecialVocab(self.dir_model, load_merges=True)
1604 |         special_vocab.add_to_gguf(self.gguf_writer)
1605 | 
1606 |     def _set_vocab_qwen(self):
1607 |         dir_model = self.dir_model
1608 |         hparams = self.hparams
1609 |         tokens: list[str] = []
1610 |         toktypes: list[int] = []
1611 | 
1612 |         from transformers import AutoTokenizer
1613 |         tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(dir_model, trust_remote_code=True)
1614 |         vocab_size = hparams["vocab_size"]
1615 |         assert max(tokenizer.get_vocab().values()) < vocab_size  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]
1616 | 
1617 |         tokpre = self.get_vocab_base_pre(tokenizer)
1618 | 
1619 |         merges = []
1620 |         vocab = {}
1621 |         mergeable_ranks = tokenizer.mergeable_ranks  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]
1622 |         for token, rank in mergeable_ranks.items():
1623 |             vocab[QwenModel.token_bytes_to_string(token)] = rank
1624 |             if len(token) == 1:
1625 |                 continue
1626 |             merged = QwenModel.bpe(mergeable_ranks, token, max_rank=rank)
1627 |             assert len(merged) == 2
1628 |             merges.append(' '.join(map(QwenModel.token_bytes_to_string, merged)))
1629 | 
1630 |         # for this kind of tokenizer, added_vocab is not a subset of vocab, so they need to be combined
1631 |         added_vocab = tokenizer.special_tokens  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]
1632 |         reverse_vocab = {id_ : encoded_tok for encoded_tok, id_ in {**vocab, **added_vocab}.items()}
1633 | 
1634 |         for i in range(vocab_size):
1635 |             if i not in reverse_vocab:
1636 |                 tokens.append(f"[PAD{i}]")
1637 |                 toktypes.append(gguf.TokenType.UNUSED)
1638 |             elif reverse_vocab[i] in added_vocab:
1639 |                 tokens.append(reverse_vocab[i])
1640 |                 toktypes.append(gguf.TokenType.CONTROL)
1641 |             else:
1642 |                 tokens.append(reverse_vocab[i])
1643 |                 toktypes.append(gguf.TokenType.NORMAL)
1644 | 
1645 |         self.gguf_writer.add_tokenizer_model("gpt2")
1646 |         self.gguf_writer.add_tokenizer_pre(tokpre)
1647 |         self.gguf_writer.add_token_list(tokens)
1648 |         self.gguf_writer.add_token_types(toktypes)
1649 | 
1650 |         special_vocab = gguf.SpecialVocab(dir_model, load_merges=False)
1651 |         special_vocab.merges = merges
1652 |         # only add special tokens when they were not already loaded from config.json
1653 |         if len(special_vocab.special_token_ids) == 0:
1654 |             special_vocab._set_special_token("bos", tokenizer.special_tokens["<|endoftext|>"])  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]
1655 |             special_vocab._set_special_token("eos", tokenizer.special_tokens["<|endoftext|>"])  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]
1656 |         # this one is usually not in config.json anyway
1657 |         special_vocab._set_special_token("unk", tokenizer.special_tokens["<|endoftext|>"])  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]
1658 |         special_vocab.add_to_gguf(self.gguf_writer)
1659 | 
1660 |     def _set_vocab_sentencepiece(self, add_to_gguf=True):
1661 |         tokens, scores, toktypes = self._create_vocab_sentencepiece()
1662 | 
1663 |         self.gguf_writer.add_tokenizer_model("llama")
1664 |         self.gguf_writer.add_tokenizer_pre("default")
1665 |         self.gguf_writer.add_token_list(tokens)
1666 |         self.gguf_writer.add_token_scores(scores)
1667 |         self.gguf_writer.add_token_types(toktypes)
1668 | 
1669 |         special_vocab = gguf.SpecialVocab(self.dir_model, n_vocab=len(tokens))
1670 |         special_vocab.add_to_gguf(self.gguf_writer)
1671 | 
1672 |     def _create_vocab_sentencepiece(self):
1673 |         from sentencepiece import SentencePieceProcessor
1674 | 
1675 |         tokenizer_path = self.dir_model / 'tokenizer.model'
1676 | 
1677 |         if not tokenizer_path.is_file():
1678 |             raise FileNotFoundError(f"File not found: {tokenizer_path}")
1679 | 
1680 |         tokenizer = SentencePieceProcessor()
1681 |         tokenizer.LoadFromFile(str(tokenizer_path))
1682 | 
1683 |         vocab_size = self.find_hparam([
1684 |             "vocab_size_per_layer_input", # gemma3n
1685 |             "vocab_size",
1686 |         ], optional=True) or tokenizer.vocab_size()
1687 | 
1688 |         tokens: list[bytes] = [f"[PAD{i}]".encode("utf-8") for i in range(vocab_size)]
1689 |         scores: list[float] = [-10000.0] * vocab_size
1690 |         toktypes: list[int] = [SentencePieceTokenTypes.UNUSED] * vocab_size
1691 | 
1692 |         for token_id in range(tokenizer.vocab_size()):
1693 |             if token_id >= vocab_size:
1694 |                 logger.warning(f'ignore tokens from {token_id}: id is out of range, max={vocab_size - 1}')
1695 |                 break
1696 | 
1697 |             piece = tokenizer.IdToPiece(token_id)
1698 |             text = piece.encode("utf-8")
1699 |             score = tokenizer.GetScore(token_id)
1700 | 
1701 |             toktype = SentencePieceTokenTypes.NORMAL
1702 |             if tokenizer.IsUnknown(token_id):
1703 |                 toktype = SentencePieceTokenTypes.UNKNOWN
1704 |             elif tokenizer.IsControl(token_id):
1705 |                 toktype = SentencePieceTokenTypes.CONTROL
1706 |             elif tokenizer.IsUnused(token_id):
1707 |                 toktype = SentencePieceTokenTypes.UNUSED
1708 |             elif tokenizer.IsByte(token_id):
1709 |                 toktype = SentencePieceTokenTypes.BYTE
1710 | 
1711 |             tokens[token_id] = text
1712 |             scores[token_id] = score
1713 |             toktypes[token_id] = toktype
1714 | 
1715 |         added_tokens_file = self.dir_model / 'added_tokens.json'
1716 |         if added_tokens_file.is_file():
1717 |             with open(added_tokens_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
1718 |                 added_tokens_json = json.load(f)
1719 |                 for key in added_tokens_json:
1720 |                     token_id = added_tokens_json[key]
1721 |                     if token_id >= vocab_size:
1722 |                         logger.warning(f'ignore token {token_id}: id is out of range, max={vocab_size - 1}')
1723 |                         continue
1724 | 
1725 |                     tokens[token_id] = key.encode("utf-8")
1726 |                     scores[token_id] = -1000.0
1727 |                     toktypes[token_id] = SentencePieceTokenTypes.USER_DEFINED
1728 | 
1729 |         tokenizer_config_file = self.dir_model / 'tokenizer_config.json'
1730 |         if tokenizer_config_file.is_file():
1731 |             with open(tokenizer_config_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
1732 |                 tokenizer_config_json = json.load(f)
1733 |                 added_tokens_decoder = tokenizer_config_json.get("added_tokens_decoder", {})
1734 |                 for token_id, token_data in added_tokens_decoder.items():
1735 |                     token_id = int(token_id)
1736 |                     token: str = token_data["content"]
1737 |                     if token_id >= vocab_size:
1738 |                         logger.warning(f'ignore token {token_id}: id is out of range, max={vocab_size - 1}')
1739 |                         continue
1740 |                     if toktypes[token_id] != SentencePieceTokenTypes.UNUSED:
1741 |                         if tokens[token_id] != token.encode("utf-8"):
1742 |                             logger.warning(f'replacing token {token_id}: {tokens[token_id].decode("utf-8")!r} -> {token!r}')
1743 |                     if token_data.get("special") or self.does_token_look_special(token):
1744 |                         toktypes[token_id] = SentencePieceTokenTypes.CONTROL
1745 |                     else:
1746 |                         token = token.replace(b"\xe2\x96\x81".decode("utf-8"), " ")  # pre-normalize user-defined spaces
1747 |                         toktypes[token_id] = SentencePieceTokenTypes.USER_DEFINED
1748 | 
1749 |                     scores[token_id] = -1000.0
1750 |                     tokens[token_id] = token.encode("utf-8")
1751 | 
1752 |         if vocab_size > len(tokens):
1753 |             pad_count = vocab_size - len(tokens)
1754 |             logger.debug(f"Padding vocab with {pad_count} token(s) - [PAD1] through [PAD{pad_count}]")
1755 |             for i in range(1, pad_count + 1):
1756 |                 tokens.append(bytes(f"[PAD{i}]", encoding="utf-8"))
1757 |                 scores.append(-1000.0)
1758 |                 toktypes.append(SentencePieceTokenTypes.UNUSED)
1759 | 
1760 |         return tokens, scores, toktypes
1761 | 
1762 |     def _set_vocab_llama_hf(self):
1763 |         vocab = gguf.LlamaHfVocab(self.dir_model)
1764 |         tokens = []
1765 |         scores = []
1766 |         toktypes = []
1767 | 
1768 |         for text, score, toktype in vocab.all_tokens():
1769 |             tokens.append(text)
1770 |             scores.append(score)
1771 |             toktypes.append(toktype)
1772 | 
1773 |         assert len(tokens) == vocab.vocab_size
1774 | 
1775 |         self.gguf_writer.add_tokenizer_model("llama")
1776 |         self.gguf_writer.add_tokenizer_pre("default")
1777 |         self.gguf_writer.add_token_list(tokens)
1778 |         self.gguf_writer.add_token_scores(scores)
1779 |         self.gguf_writer.add_token_types(toktypes)
1780 | 
1781 |         special_vocab = gguf.SpecialVocab(self.dir_model, n_vocab=len(tokens))
1782 |         special_vocab.add_to_gguf(self.gguf_writer)
1783 | 
1784 |     def _set_vocab_rwkv_world(self):
1785 |         assert (self.dir_model / "rwkv_vocab_v20230424.txt").is_file()
1786 |         vocab_size = self.hparams.get("vocab_size", 65536)
1787 | 
1788 |         tokens: list[bytes] = ['<s>'.encode("utf-8")]
1789 |         toktypes: list[int] = [gguf.TokenType.CONTROL]
1790 | 
1791 |         with open(self.dir_model / "rwkv_vocab_v20230424.txt", "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
1792 |             lines = f.readlines()
1793 |             for line in lines:
1794 |                 parts = line.split(' ')
1795 |                 assert len(parts) >= 3
1796 |                 token, token_len = ast.literal_eval(' '.join(parts[1:-1])), int(parts[-1])
1797 |                 token = token.encode("utf-8") if isinstance(token, str) else token
1798 |                 assert isinstance(token, bytes)
1799 |                 assert len(token) == token_len
1800 |                 token_text: str = repr(token)[2:-1]  # "b'\xff'" -> "\xff"
1801 |                 tokens.append(token_text.encode("utf-8"))
1802 |                 toktypes.append(gguf.TokenType.NORMAL)
1803 |         remainder = vocab_size - len(tokens)
1804 |         assert remainder >= 0
1805 |         for i in range(len(tokens), vocab_size):
1806 |             tokens.append(f"[PAD{i}]".encode("utf-8"))
1807 |             toktypes.append(gguf.TokenType.UNUSED)
1808 | 
1809 |         self.gguf_writer.add_tokenizer_model("rwkv")
1810 |         self.gguf_writer.add_token_list(tokens)
1811 |         self.gguf_writer.add_token_types(toktypes)
1812 |         special_vocab = gguf.SpecialVocab(self.dir_model, load_merges=False)
1813 |         if special_vocab.chat_template is None:
1814 |             template_path = Path(__file__).parent / "models" / "templates" / "llama-cpp-rwkv-world.jinja"
1815 |             if template_path.is_file():
1816 |                 with open(template_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
1817 |                     template = f.read()
1818 |             else:
1819 |                 template = "rwkv-world"
1820 |             special_vocab.chat_template = template
1821 |         # hack: Add '\n\n' as the EOT token to make it chat normally
1822 |         special_vocab._set_special_token("eot", 261)
1823 |         # hack: Override these as they have already been set (incorrectly)
1824 |         special_vocab.special_token_ids["bos"] = 0
1825 |         special_vocab.special_token_ids["eos"] = 0
1826 | 
1827 |         special_vocab.add_to_gguf(self.gguf_writer)
1828 | 
1829 |     def _set_vocab_builtin(self, model_name: Literal["gpt-neox", "llama-spm"], vocab_size: int):
1830 |         tokenizer_path = Path(sys.path[0]) / "models" / f"ggml-vocab-{model_name}.gguf"
1831 |         logger.warning(f"Using tokenizer from '{os.path.relpath(tokenizer_path, os.getcwd())}'")
1832 |         vocab_reader = gguf.GGUFReader(tokenizer_path, "r")
1833 | 
1834 |         default_pre = "mpt" if model_name == "gpt-neox" else "default"
1835 | 
1836 |         field = vocab_reader.get_field(gguf.Keys.Tokenizer.MODEL)
1837 |         assert field  # tokenizer model
1838 |         self.gguf_writer.add_tokenizer_model(bytes(field.parts[-1]).decode("utf-8"))
1839 | 
1840 |         field = vocab_reader.get_field(gguf.Keys.Tokenizer.PRE)
1841 |         self.gguf_writer.add_tokenizer_pre(bytes(field.parts[-1]).decode("utf-8") if field else default_pre)
1842 | 
1843 |         field = vocab_reader.get_field(gguf.Keys.Tokenizer.LIST)
1844 |         assert field  # token list
1845 |         self.gguf_writer.add_token_list([bytes(field.parts[i]) for i in field.data][:vocab_size])
1846 | 
1847 |         if model_name == "llama-spm":
1848 |             field = vocab_reader.get_field(gguf.Keys.Tokenizer.SCORES)
1849 |             assert field  # token scores
1850 |             self.gguf_writer.add_token_scores([field.parts[i].tolist()[0] for i in field.data][:vocab_size])
1851 | 
1852 |         field = vocab_reader.get_field(gguf.Keys.Tokenizer.TOKEN_TYPE)
1853 |         assert field  # token types
1854 |         self.gguf_writer.add_token_types([field.parts[i].tolist()[0] for i in field.data][:vocab_size])
1855 | 
1856 |         if model_name != "llama-spm":
1857 |             field = vocab_reader.get_field(gguf.Keys.Tokenizer.MERGES)
1858 |             assert field  # token merges
1859 |             self.gguf_writer.add_token_merges([bytes(field.parts[i]) for i in field.data])
1860 | 
1861 |         if (field := vocab_reader.get_field(gguf.Keys.Tokenizer.BOS_ID)) is not None:
1862 |             self.gguf_writer.add_bos_token_id(field.parts[-1].tolist()[0])
1863 |         if (field := vocab_reader.get_field(gguf.Keys.Tokenizer.EOS_ID)) is not None:
1864 |             self.gguf_writer.add_eos_token_id(field.parts[-1].tolist()[0])
1865 |         if (field := vocab_reader.get_field(gguf.Keys.Tokenizer.UNK_ID)) is not None:
1866 |             self.gguf_writer.add_unk_token_id(field.parts[-1].tolist()[0])
1867 |         if (field := vocab_reader.get_field(gguf.Keys.Tokenizer.PAD_ID)) is not None:
1868 |             self.gguf_writer.add_pad_token_id(field.parts[-1].tolist()[0])
1869 |         if (field := vocab_reader.get_field(gguf.Keys.Tokenizer.ADD_BOS)) is not None:
1870 |             self.gguf_writer.add_add_bos_token(field.parts[-1].tolist()[0])
1871 |         if (field := vocab_reader.get_field(gguf.Keys.Tokenizer.ADD_EOS)) is not None:
1872 |             self.gguf_writer.add_add_eos_token(field.parts[-1].tolist()[0])
1873 | 
1874 |     def _try_set_pooling_type(self) -> None:
1875 |         # get pooling path
1876 |         pooling_path = None
1877 |         module_path = self.dir_model / "modules.json"
1878 |         if module_path.is_file():
1879 |             with open(module_path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
1880 |                 modules = json.load(f)
1881 |             for mod in modules:
1882 |                 if mod["type"].endswith("Pooling"):
1883 |                     pooling_path = mod["path"]
1884 |                     break
1885 | 
1886 |         mode_mapping = {
1887 |             "mean": gguf.PoolingType.MEAN,
1888 |             "cls": gguf.PoolingType.CLS,
1889 |             "lasttoken": gguf.PoolingType.LAST,
1890 |         }
1891 | 
1892 |         # get pooling type
1893 |         if pooling_path is not None:
1894 |             with open(self.dir_model / pooling_path / "config.json", encoding="utf-8") as f:
1895 |                 pooling = json.load(f)
1896 |             if pooling.get("pooling_mode_mean_tokens"):
1897 |                 pooling_type = gguf.PoolingType.MEAN
1898 |             elif pooling.get("pooling_mode_cls_token"):
1899 |                 pooling_type = gguf.PoolingType.CLS
1900 |             elif pooling.get("pooling_mode_lasttoken"):
1901 |                 pooling_type = gguf.PoolingType.LAST
1902 |             elif (pooling_mode := pooling.get("pooling_mode")) in mode_mapping:
1903 |                 pooling_type = mode_mapping[pooling_mode]
1904 |             else:
1905 |                 raise NotImplementedError("Only MEAN, CLS, and LAST pooling types supported")
1906 |             self.gguf_writer.add_pooling_type(pooling_type)
1907 | 
1908 |     def _set_vocab_glmedge(self):
1909 |         from transformers import AutoTokenizer
1910 |         tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(self.dir_model)
1911 |         special_vocab = gguf.SpecialVocab(self.dir_model, load_merges=True)
1912 |         tokens, toktypes, tokpre = self.get_vocab_base()
1913 |         self.gguf_writer.add_tokenizer_model("gpt2")
1914 |         self.gguf_writer.add_tokenizer_pre(tokpre)
1915 |         self.gguf_writer.add_token_list(tokens)
1916 |         self.gguf_writer.add_token_types(toktypes)
1917 |         special_vocab._set_special_token("eos", tokenizer.get_added_vocab()["<|endoftext|>"])  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]
1918 |         special_vocab._set_special_token("eot", tokenizer.get_added_vocab()["<|user|>"])  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]
1919 |         special_vocab._set_special_token("unk", tokenizer.get_added_vocab()["<|endoftext|>"])  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]
1920 |         special_vocab._set_special_token("bos", tokenizer.get_added_vocab()["<|endoftext|>"])  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]
1921 |         special_vocab.add_to_gguf(self.gguf_writer)
1922 | 
1923 |     def _set_vocab_glm(self):
1924 |         from transformers import AutoTokenizer
1925 |         tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(self.dir_model)
1926 |         special_vocab = gguf.SpecialVocab(self.dir_model, load_merges=True)
1927 |         tokens, toktypes, tokpre = self.get_vocab_base()
1928 |         self.gguf_writer.add_tokenizer_model("gpt2")
1929 |         self.gguf_writer.add_tokenizer_pre(tokpre)
1930 |         self.gguf_writer.add_token_list(tokens)
1931 |         self.gguf_writer.add_token_types(toktypes)
1932 |         # Special tokens
1933 |         # Note: Using <|endoftext|> (151329) for eot causes endless generation
1934 |         special_vocab._set_special_token("bos", tokenizer.get_added_vocab()["[gMASK]"])  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]  # 151331
1935 |         special_vocab._set_special_token("eot", tokenizer.get_added_vocab()["<|user|>"])  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]  # 151336
1936 |         special_vocab._set_special_token("unk", tokenizer.get_added_vocab()["<|endoftext|>"])  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]  # 151329
1937 |         special_vocab._set_special_token("eom", tokenizer.get_added_vocab()["<|observation|>"])  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]  # 151338
1938 |         special_vocab.add_to_gguf(self.gguf_writer)
1939 | 
1940 |     def _set_vocab_interns1(self):
1941 |         tokens: list[str] = []
1942 |         toktypes: list[int] = []
1943 | 
1944 |         from transformers import AutoTokenizer
1945 |         tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(self.dir_model, trust_remote_code=True)
1946 |         vocab = getattr(tokenizer, 'vocab', tokenizer.get_vocab())  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]
1947 |         vocab_size = self.hparams.get("vocab_size", len(vocab))
1948 |         assert max(vocab.values()) < vocab_size
1949 | 
1950 |         tokpre = self.get_vocab_base_pre(tokenizer)
1951 | 
1952 |         reverse_vocab = {id_: encoded_tok for encoded_tok, id_ in vocab.items()}
1953 |         added_vocab = tokenizer.get_added_vocab()  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]
1954 | 
1955 |         added_tokens_decoder = tokenizer.added_tokens_decoder  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute]
1956 | 
1957 |         for i in range(vocab_size):
1958 |             if i not in reverse_vocab:
1959 |                 tokens.append(f"[PAD{i}]")
1960 |                 toktypes.append(gguf.TokenType.UNUSED)
1961 |             else:
1962 |                 token: str = reverse_vocab[i]
1963 |                 if token in added_vocab:
1964 |                     # The tokenizer in llama.cpp assumes the CONTROL and USER_DEFINED tokens are pre-normalized.
1965 |                     # To avoid unexpected issues - we make sure to normalize non-normalized tokens
1966 |                     if not added_tokens_decoder[i].normalized:
1967 |                         previous_token = token
1968 |                         token = tokenizer.decode(tokenizer.encode(token, add_special_tokens=False))  # ty: ignore[unresolved-attribute, invalid-assignment]
1969 |                         if previous_token != token:
1970 |                             logger.info(f"{repr(previous_token)} is encoded and decoded back to {repr(token)} using AutoTokenizer")
1971 | 
1972 |                     if added_tokens_decoder[i].special or self.does_token_look_special(token):
1973 |                         toktypes.append(gguf.TokenType.CONTROL)
1974 |                     else:
1975 |                         toktypes.append(gguf.TokenType.USER_DEFINED)
1976 |                 else:
1977 |                     toktypes.append(gguf.TokenType.NORMAL)
1978 |                 tokens.append(token)
1979 | 
1980 |         self.gguf_writer.add_tokenizer_model("gpt2")
1981 |         self.gguf_writer.add_tokenizer_pre(tokpre)
1982 |         self.gguf_writer.add_token_list(tokens)
1983 |         self.gguf_writer.add_token_types(toktypes)
1984 | 
1985 |         special_vocab = gguf.SpecialVocab(self.dir_model, load_merges=True)
1986 |         special_vocab._set_special_token("bos", 151643)
1987 |         special_vocab.add_to_gguf(self.gguf_writer)
1988 | 
1989 |     def _set_vocab_mistral(self):
1990 |         if not _mistral_common_installed:
1991 |             raise ImportError(_mistral_import_error_msg)
1992 | 
1993 |         vocab = MistralVocab(self.dir_model)
1994 |         logger.info(
1995 |             f"Converting tokenizer {vocab.tokenizer_type} of size {vocab.vocab_size}."
1996 |         )
1997 | 
1998 |         self.gguf_writer.add_tokenizer_model(vocab.gguf_tokenizer_model)
1999 | 
2000 |         tokens = []

  34 | ###### MODEL DEFINITIONS ######
  35 | 
  36 | class SentencePieceTokenTypes(IntEnum):
  37 |     NORMAL = 1
  38 |     UNKNOWN = 2
  39 |     CONTROL = 3

  42 |     BYTE = 6

  45 | AnyModel = TypeVar("AnyModel", bound="type[Model]")

  47 | 
  48 | class Model:
  49 |     _model_classes: dict[str, type[Model]] = {}
  50 | 
  51 |     dir_model: Path
  52 |     ftype: gguf.LlamaFileType
  53 |     fname_out: Path
  54 |     is_big_endian: bool
  55 |     endianess: gguf.GGUFEndian
  56 |     use_temp_file: bool
  57 |     lazy: bool
  58 |     part_names: list[str]
  59 |     is_safetensors: bool
  60 |     hparams: dict[str, Any]
  61 |     block_count: int
  62 |     tensor_map: gguf.TensorNameMap
  63 |     tensor_names: set[str] | None
  64 |     gguf_writer: gguf.GGUFWriter
  65 |     model_name: str | None
  66 |     metadata_override: Path | None
  67 |     dir_model_card: Path
  68 | 
  69 |     # subclasses should define this!
  70 |     model_arch: gguf.MODEL_ARCH
  71 | 
  72 |     def __init__(self, dir_model: Path, ftype: gguf.LlamaFileType, fname_out: Path, is_big_endian: bool = False,
  73 |                  use_temp_file: bool = False, eager: bool = False,
  74 |                  metadata_override: Path | None = None, model_name: str | None = None,
  75 |                  split_max_tensors: int = 0, split_max_size: int = 0, dry_run: bool = False, small_first_shard: bool = False):
  76 |         if type(self) is Model:
  77 |             raise TypeError(f"{type(self).__name__!r} should not be directly instantiated")
  78 | 
  79 |         self.dir_model = dir_model
  80 |         self.ftype = ftype
  81 |         self.fname_out = fname_out
  82 |         self.is_big_endian = is_big_endian
  83 |         self.endianess = gguf.GGUFEndian.BIG if is_big_endian else gguf.GGUFEndian.LITTLE
  84 |         self.use_temp_file = use_temp_file
  85 |         self.lazy = not eager
  86 |         self.part_names = Model.get_model_part_names(self.dir_model, "model", ".safetensors")
  87 |         self.is_safetensors = len(self.part_names) > 0
  88 |         if not self.is_safetensors:
  89 |             self.part_names = Model.get_model_part_names(self.dir_model, "pytorch_model", ".bin")
  90 |         self.hparams = Model.load_hparams(self.dir_model)
  91 |         self.block_count = self.find_hparam(["n_layers", "num_hidden_layers", "n_layer", "num_layers"])
  92 |         self.tensor_map = gguf.get_tensor_name_map(self.model_arch, self.block_count)
  93 |         self.tensor_names = None
  94 |         self.metadata_override = metadata_override
  95 |         self.model_name = model_name
  96 |         self.dir_model_card = dir_model  # overridden in convert_lora_to_gguf.py
  97 | 
  98 |         # Apply heuristics to figure out typical tensor encoding based on first layer tensor encoding type
  99 |         if self.ftype == gguf.LlamaFileType.GUESSED:
 100 |             # NOTE: can't use field "torch_dtype" in config.json, because some finetunes lie.
 101 |             _, first_tensor = next(self.get_tensors())
 102 |             if first_tensor.dtype == torch.float16:
 103 |                 logger.info(f"choosing --outtype f16 from first tensor type ({first_tensor.dtype})")
 104 |                 self.ftype = gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_F16
 105 |             else:
 106 |                 logger.info(f"choosing --outtype bf16 from first tensor type ({first_tensor.dtype})")
 107 |                 self.ftype = gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_BF16
 108 | 
 109 |         # Configure GGUF Writer
 110 |         self.gguf_writer = gguf.GGUFWriter(path=None, arch=gguf.MODEL_ARCH_NAMES[self.model_arch], endianess=self.endianess, use_temp_file=self.use_temp_file,
 111 |                                            split_max_tensors=split_max_tensors, split_max_size=split_max_size, dry_run=dry_run, small_first_shard=small_first_shard)
 112 | 
 113 |     @classmethod
 114 |     def __init_subclass__(cls):
 115 |         # can't use an abstract property, because overriding it without type errors
 116 |         # would require using decorated functions instead of simply defining the property
 117 |         if "model_arch" not in cls.__dict__:
 118 |             raise TypeError(f"Missing property 'model_arch' for {cls.__name__!r}")
 119 | 
 120 |     def find_hparam(self, keys: Iterable[str], optional: bool = False) -> Any:
 121 |         key = next((k for k in keys if k in self.hparams), None)
 122 |         if key is not None:
 123 |             return self.hparams[key]
 124 |         if optional:
 125 |             return None
 126 |         raise KeyError(f"could not find any of: {keys}")
 127 | 
 128 |     def set_vocab(self):
 129 |         self._set_vocab_gpt2()
 130 | 
 131 |     def get_tensors(self) -> Iterator[tuple[str, Tensor]]:
 132 |         tensor_names_from_parts: set[str] = set()
 133 | 
 134 |         index_name = "model.safetensors" if self.is_safetensors else "pytorch_model.bin"
 135 |         index_name += ".index.json"
 136 |         index_file = self.dir_model / index_name
 137 | 
 138 |         if index_file.is_file():
 139 |             self.tensor_names = set()
 140 |             logger.info(f"gguf: loading model weight map from '{index_name}'")
 141 |             with open(index_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
 142 |                 index: dict[str, Any] = json.load(f)
 143 |                 weight_map = index.get("weight_map")
 144 |                 if weight_map is None or not isinstance(weight_map, dict):
 145 |                     raise ValueError(f"Can't load 'weight_map' from {index_name!r}")
 146 |                 self.tensor_names.update(weight_map.keys())
 147 |         else:
 148 |             self.tensor_names = tensor_names_from_parts
 149 |             weight_map = {}
 150 | 
 151 |         for part_name in self.part_names:
 152 |             logger.info(f"gguf: loading model part '{part_name}'")
 153 |             ctx: ContextManager[Any]
 154 |             if self.is_safetensors:
 155 |                 from safetensors import safe_open
 156 |                 ctx = cast(ContextManager[Any], safe_open(self.dir_model / part_name, framework="pt", device="cpu"))
 157 |             else:
 158 |                 ctx = contextlib.nullcontext(torch.load(str(self.dir_model / part_name), map_location="cpu", mmap=True, weights_only=True))
 159 | 
 160 |             with ctx as model_part:
 161 |                 tensor_names_from_parts.update(model_part.keys())
 162 | 
 163 |                 for name in model_part.keys():
 164 |                     if self.is_safetensors:
 165 |                         if self.lazy:
 166 |                             data = model_part.get_slice(name)
 167 |                             data = LazyTorchTensor.from_safetensors_slice(data)
 168 |                         else:
 169 |                             data = model_part.get_tensor(name)
 170 |                     else:
 171 |                         data = model_part[name]
 172 |                         if self.lazy:
 173 |                             data = LazyTorchTensor.from_eager(data)
 174 |                     yield name, data
 175 | 
 176 |         # verify tensor name presence and identify potentially missing files
 177 |         if len(tensor_names_from_parts.symmetric_difference(self.tensor_names)) > 0:
 178 |             missing = sorted(self.tensor_names.difference(tensor_names_from_parts))
 179 |             extra = sorted(tensor_names_from_parts.difference(self.tensor_names))
 180 |             missing_files = sorted(set(weight_map[n] for n in missing if n in weight_map))
 181 |             if len(extra) == 0 and len(missing_files) > 0:
 182 |                 raise ValueError(f"Missing or incomplete model files: {missing_files}")
 183 |             else:
 184 |                 raise ValueError("Mismatch between weight map and model parts for tensor names:\n"
 185 |                                  f"Missing tensors: {missing}\n"
 186 |                                  f"Extra tensors: {extra}")
 187 | 
 188 |     def format_tensor_name(self, key: gguf.MODEL_TENSOR, bid: int | None = None, suffix: str = ".weight") -> str:
 189 |         if key not in gguf.MODEL_TENSORS[self.model_arch]:
 190 |             raise ValueError(f"Missing {key!r} for MODEL_TENSORS of {self.model_arch!r}")
 191 |         name: str = gguf.TENSOR_NAMES[key]
 192 |         if "{bid}" in name:
 193 |             assert bid is not None
 194 |             name = name.format(bid=bid)
 195 |         return name + suffix
 196 | 
 197 |     def match_model_tensor_name(self, name: str, key: gguf.MODEL_TENSOR, bid: int | None, suffix: str = ".weight") -> bool:
 198 |         if key not in gguf.MODEL_TENSORS[self.model_arch]:
 199 |             return False
 200 |         key_name: str = gguf.TENSOR_NAMES[key]
 201 |         if "{bid}" in key_name:
 202 |             if bid is None:
 203 |                 return False
 204 |             key_name = key_name.format(bid=bid)
 205 |         else:
 206 |             if bid is not None:
 207 |                 return False
 208 |         return name == (key_name + suffix)
 209 | 
 210 |     def map_tensor_name(self, name: str, try_suffixes: Sequence[str] = (".weight", ".bias")) -> str:
 211 |         new_name = self.tensor_map.get_name(key=name, try_suffixes=try_suffixes)
 212 |         if new_name is None:
 213 |             raise ValueError(f"Can not map tensor {name!r}")
 214 |         return new_name
 215 | 
 216 |     def set_gguf_parameters(self):
 217 |         self.gguf_writer.add_block_count(self.block_count)
 218 | 
 219 |         if (n_ctx := self.find_hparam(["max_position_embeddings", "n_ctx"], optional=True)) is not None:
 220 |             self.gguf_writer.add_context_length(n_ctx)
 221 |             logger.info(f"gguf: context length = {n_ctx}")
 222 | 
 223 |         n_embd = self.find_hparam(["hidden_size", "n_embd"])
 224 |         self.gguf_writer.add_embedding_length(n_embd)
 225 |         logger.info(f"gguf: embedding length = {n_embd}")
 226 | 
 227 |         if (n_ff := self.find_hparam(["intermediate_size", "n_inner"], optional=True)) is not None:
 228 |             self.gguf_writer.add_feed_forward_length(n_ff)
 229 |             logger.info(f"gguf: feed forward length = {n_ff}")
 230 | 
 231 |         n_head = self.find_hparam(["num_attention_heads", "n_head"])
 232 |         self.gguf_writer.add_head_count(n_head)
 233 |         logger.info(f"gguf: head count = {n_head}")
 234 | 
 235 |         if (n_head_kv := self.hparams.get("num_key_value_heads")) is not None:
 236 |             self.gguf_writer.add_head_count_kv(n_head_kv)
 237 |             logger.info(f"gguf: key-value head count = {n_head_kv}")
 238 | 
 239 |         if (rope_theta := self.hparams.get("rope_theta")) is not None:
 240 |             self.gguf_writer.add_rope_freq_base(rope_theta)
 241 |             logger.info(f"gguf: rope theta = {rope_theta}")
 242 |         if (f_rms_eps := self.hparams.get("rms_norm_eps")) is not None:
 243 |             self.gguf_writer.add_layer_norm_rms_eps(f_rms_eps)
 244 |             logger.info(f"gguf: rms norm epsilon = {f_rms_eps}")
 245 |         if (f_norm_eps := self.find_hparam(["layer_norm_eps", "layer_norm_epsilon", "norm_epsilon"], optional=True)) is not None:
 246 |             self.gguf_writer.add_layer_norm_eps(f_norm_eps)
 247 |             logger.info(f"gguf: layer norm epsilon = {f_norm_eps}")
 248 |         if (n_experts := self.hparams.get("num_local_experts")) is not None:
 249 |             self.gguf_writer.add_expert_count(n_experts)
 250 |             logger.info(f"gguf: expert count = {n_experts}")
 251 |         if (n_experts_used := self.hparams.get("num_experts_per_tok")) is not None:
 252 |             self.gguf_writer.add_expert_used_count(n_experts_used)
 253 |             logger.info(f"gguf: experts used count = {n_experts_used}")
 254 | 
 255 |         if (head_dim := self.hparams.get("head_dim")) is not None:
 256 |             self.gguf_writer.add_key_length(head_dim)
 257 |             self.gguf_writer.add_value_length(head_dim)
 258 | 
 259 |         self.gguf_writer.add_file_type(self.ftype)
 260 |         logger.info(f"gguf: file type = {self.ftype}")
 261 | 
 262 |     def modify_tensors(self, data_torch: Tensor, name: str, bid: int | None) -> Iterable[tuple[str, Tensor]]:
 263 |         del bid  # unused
 264 | 
 265 |         return [(self.map_tensor_name(name), data_torch)]
 266 | 
 267 |     def tensor_force_quant(self, name: str, new_name: str, bid: int | None, n_dims: int) -> gguf.GGMLQuantizationType | bool:
 268 |         del name, new_name, bid, n_dims  # unused
 269 | 
 270 |         return False
 271 | 
 272 |     # some models need extra generated tensors (like rope_freqs)
 273 |     def generate_extra_tensors(self) -> Iterable[tuple[str, Tensor]]:
 274 |         return ()
 275 | 
 276 |     def prepare_tensors(self):
 277 |         max_name_len = max(len(s) for _, s in self.tensor_map.mapping.values()) + len(".weight,")
 278 | 
 279 |         for name, data_torch in chain(self.generate_extra_tensors(), self.get_tensors()):
 280 |             # we don't need these
 281 |             if name.endswith((".attention.masked_bias", ".attention.bias", ".rotary_emb.inv_freq")):
 282 |                 continue
 283 | 
 284 |             old_dtype = data_torch.dtype
 285 | 
 286 |             # convert any unsupported data types to float32
 287 |             if data_torch.dtype not in (torch.float16, torch.float32):
 288 |                 data_torch = data_torch.to(torch.float32)
 289 | 
 290 |             # use the first number-like part of the tensor name as the block id
 291 |             bid = None
 292 |             for part in name.split("."):
 293 |                 if part.isdecimal():
 294 |                     bid = int(part)
 295 |                     break
 296 | 
 297 |             for new_name, data_torch in (self.modify_tensors(data_torch, name, bid)):
 298 |                 data = data_torch.squeeze().numpy()
 299 | 
 300 |                 # if data ends up empty, it means data_torch was a scalar tensor -> restore
 301 |                 if len(data.shape) == 0:
 302 |                     data = data_torch.numpy()
 303 | 
 304 |                 n_dims = len(data.shape)
 305 |                 data_qtype: gguf.GGMLQuantizationType | bool = self.tensor_force_quant(name, new_name, bid, n_dims)
 306 | 
 307 |                 # Most of the codebase that takes in 1D tensors or norms only handles F32 tensors
 308 |                 if n_dims <= 1 or new_name.endswith("_norm.weight"):
 309 |                     data_qtype = gguf.GGMLQuantizationType.F32
 310 | 
 311 |                 # Conditions should closely match those in llama_model_quantize_internal in llama.cpp
 312 |                 # Some tensor types are always in float32
 313 |                 if data_qtype is False and (
 314 |                     any(
 315 |                         self.match_model_tensor_name(new_name, key, bid)
 316 |                         for key in (
 317 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.FFN_GATE_INP,
 318 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.POS_EMBD,
 319 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.TOKEN_TYPES,
 320 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.SSM_CONV1D,
 321 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.TIME_MIX_FIRST,
 322 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.TIME_MIX_W1,
 323 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.TIME_MIX_W2,
 324 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.TIME_MIX_DECAY_W1,
 325 |                             gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.TIME_MIX_DECAY_W2,
 326 |                         )
 327 |                     )
 328 |                     or not new_name.endswith(".weight")
 329 |                 ):
 330 |                     data_qtype = gguf.GGMLQuantizationType.F32
 331 | 
 332 |                 if data_qtype is False and any(
 333 |                     self.match_model_tensor_name(new_name, key, bid)
 334 |                     for key in (
 335 |                         gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.TOKEN_EMBD,
 336 |                         gguf.MODEL_TENSOR.OUTPUT,
 337 |                     )
 338 |                 ):
 339 |                     if self.ftype in (
 340 |                         gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_TQ1_0,
 341 |                         gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_TQ2_0,
 342 |                     ):
 343 |                         # TODO: use Q4_K and Q6_K
 344 |                         data_qtype = gguf.GGMLQuantizationType.F16
 345 | 
 346 |                 # No override (data_qtype is False), or wants to be quantized (data_qtype is True)
 347 |                 if isinstance(data_qtype, bool):
 348 |                     if self.ftype == gguf.LlamaFileType.ALL_F32:
 349 |                         data_qtype = gguf.GGMLQuantizationType.F32
 350 |                     elif self.ftype == gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_F16:
 351 |                         data_qtype = gguf.GGMLQuantizationType.F16
 352 |                     elif self.ftype == gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_BF16:
 353 |                         data_qtype = gguf.GGMLQuantizationType.BF16
 354 |                     elif self.ftype == gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_Q8_0:
 355 |                         data_qtype = gguf.GGMLQuantizationType.Q8_0
 356 |                     elif self.ftype == gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_TQ1_0:
 357 |                         data_qtype = gguf.GGMLQuantizationType.TQ1_0
 358 |                     elif self.ftype == gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_TQ2_0:
 359 |                         data_qtype = gguf.GGMLQuantizationType.TQ2_0
 360 |                     else:
 361 |                         raise ValueError(f"Unknown file type: {self.ftype.name}")
 362 | 
 363 |                 try:
 364 |                     data = gguf.quants.quantize(data, data_qtype)
 365 |                 except gguf.QuantError as e:
 366 |                     logger.warning("%s, %s", e, "falling back to F16")
 367 |                     data_qtype = gguf.GGMLQuantizationType.F16
 368 |                     data = gguf.quants.quantize(data, data_qtype)
 369 | 
 370 |                 shape = gguf.quant_shape_from_byte_shape(data.shape, data_qtype) if data.dtype == np.uint8 else data.shape
 371 | 
 372 |                 # reverse shape to make it similar to the internal ggml dimension order
 373 |                 shape_str = f"{{{', '.join(str(n) for n in reversed(shape))}}}"
 374 | 
 375 |                 # n_dims is implicit in the shape
 376 |                 logger.info(f"{f'%-{max_name_len}s' % f'{new_name},'} {old_dtype} --> {data_qtype.name}, shape = {shape_str}")
 377 | 
 378 |                 self.gguf_writer.add_tensor(new_name, data, raw_dtype=data_qtype)
 379 | 
 380 |     def set_type(self):
 381 |         self.gguf_writer.add_type(gguf.GGUFType.MODEL)
 382 | 
 383 |     def prepare_metadata(self, vocab_only: bool):
 384 | 
 385 |         total_params, shared_params, expert_params, expert_count = self.gguf_writer.get_total_parameter_count()
 386 | 
 387 |         self.metadata = gguf.Metadata.load(self.metadata_override, self.dir_model_card, self.model_name, total_params)
 388 | 
 389 |         # Fallback to model directory name if metadata name is still missing
 390 |         if self.metadata.name is None:
 391 |             self.metadata.name = self.dir_model.name
 392 | 
 393 |         # Generate parameter weight class (useful for leader boards) if not yet determined
 394 |         if self.metadata.size_label is None and total_params > 0:
 395 |             self.metadata.size_label = gguf.size_label(total_params, shared_params, expert_params, expert_count)
 396 | 
 397 |         # Extract the encoding scheme from the file type name. e.g. 'gguf.LlamaFileType.MOSTLY_Q8_0' --> 'Q8_0'
 398 |         output_type: str = self.ftype.name.partition("_")[2]
 399 | 
 400 |         # Filename Output
 401 |         if self.fname_out.is_dir():
 402 |             # Generate default filename based on model specification and available metadata
 403 |             if not vocab_only:
 404 |                 fname_default: str = gguf.naming_convention(self.metadata.name, self.metadata.basename, self.metadata.finetune, self.metadata.version, self.metadata.size_label, output_type, model_type="LoRA" if total_params < 0 else None)
 405 |             else:
 406 |                 fname_default: str = gguf.naming_convention(self.metadata.name, self.metadata.basename, self.metadata.finetune, self.metadata.version, size_label=None, output_type=None, model_type="vocab")
 407 | 
 408 |             # Use the default filename
 409 |             self.fname_out = self.fname_out / f"{fname_default}.gguf"
 410 |         else:
 411 |             # Output path is a custom defined templated filename
 412 |             # Note: `not is_dir()` is used because `.is_file()` will not detect
 413 |             #       file template strings as it doesn't actually exist as a file
 414 | 
 415 |             # Process templated file name with the output ftype, useful with the "auto" ftype
 416 |             self.fname_out = self.fname_out.parent / gguf.fill_templated_filename(self.fname_out.name, output_type)
 417 | 
 418 |         self.set_type()
 419 | 
 420 |         logger.info("Set meta model")
 421 |         self.metadata.set_gguf_meta_model(self.gguf_writer)
 422 | 
 423 |         logger.info("Set model parameters")
 424 |         self.set_gguf_parameters()
 425 | 
 426 |         logger.info("Set model tokenizer")
 427 |         self.set_vocab()
 428 | 
 429 |         logger.info("Set model quantization version")
 430 |         self.gguf_writer.add_quantization_version(gguf.GGML_QUANT_VERSION)
 431 | 
 432 |     def write(self):
 433 |         self.prepare_tensors()
 434 |         self.prepare_metadata(vocab_only=False)
 435 |         self.gguf_writer.write_header_to_file(path=self.fname_out)
 436 |         self.gguf_writer.write_kv_data_to_file()
 437 |         self.gguf_writer.write_tensors_to_file(progress=True)
 438 |         self.gguf_writer.close()
 439 | 
 440 |     def write_vocab(self):
 441 |         if len(self.gguf_writer.tensors) != 1:
 442 |             raise ValueError('Splitting the vocabulary is not supported')
 443 | 
 444 |         self.prepare_metadata(vocab_only=True)
 445 |         self.gguf_writer.write_header_to_file(path=self.fname_out)
 446 |         self.gguf_writer.write_kv_data_to_file()
 447 |         self.gguf_writer.close()
 448 | 
 449 |     @staticmethod
 450 |     def get_model_part_names(dir_model: Path, prefix: str, suffix: str) -> list[str]:
 451 |         part_names: list[str] = []
 452 |         for filename in os.listdir(dir_model):
 453 |             if filename.startswith(prefix) and filename.endswith(suffix):
 454 |                 part_names.append(filename)
 455 | 
 456 |         part_names.sort()
 457 | 
 458 |         return part_names
 459 | 
 460 |     @staticmethod
 461 |     def load_hparams(dir_model: Path):
 462 |         with open(dir_model / "config.json", "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
 463 |             return json.load(f)
 464 | 
 465 |     @classmethod
 466 |     def register(cls, *names: str) -> Callable[[AnyModel], AnyModel]:
 467 |         assert names
 468 | 
 469 |         def func(modelcls: AnyModel) -> AnyModel:
 470 |             for name in names:
 471 |                 cls._model_classes[name] = modelcls
 472 |             return modelcls
 473 |         return func
 474 | 
 475 |     @classmethod
 476 |     def from_model_architecture(cls, arch: str) -> type[Model]:
 477 |         try:
 478 |             return cls._model_classes[arch]
 479 |         except KeyError:
 480 |             raise NotImplementedError(f'Architecture {arch!r} not supported!') from None
 481 | 
 482 |     def does_token_look_special(self, token: str | bytes) -> bool:
 483 |         if isinstance(token, (bytes, bytearray)):
 484 |             token_text = token.decode(encoding="utf-8")
 485 |         elif isinstance(token, memoryview):
 486 |             token_text = token.tobytes().decode(encoding="utf-8")
 487 |         else:
 488 |             token_text = token
 489 | 
 490 |         # Some models mark some added tokens which ought to be control tokens as not special.
 491 |         # (e.g. command-r, command-r-plus, deepseek-coder, gemma{,-2})
 492 |         seems_special = token_text in (
 493 |             "<pad>",  # deepseek-coder
 494 |             "<mask>", "<2mass>", "[@BOS@]",  # gemma{,-2}
 495 |         )
 496 | 
 497 |         seems_special = seems_special or (token_text.startswith("<|") and token_text.endswith("|>"))
 498 |         seems_special = seems_special or (token_text.startswith("<｜") and token_text.endswith("｜>"))  # deepseek-coder
 499 | 
 500 |         # TODO: should these be marked as UNUSED instead? (maybe not)
 501 |         seems_special = seems_special or (token_text.startswith("<unused") and token_text.endswith(">"))  # gemma{,-2}
 502 | 
 503 |         return seems_special
 504 | 
 505 |     # used for GPT-2 BPE and WordPiece vocabs
 506 |     def get_vocab_base(self) -> tuple[list[str], list[int], str]:
 507 |         tokens: list[str] = []
 508 |         toktypes: list[int] = []
 509 | 
 510 |         from transformers import AutoTokenizer
 511 |         tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(self.dir_model)
 512 |         vocab_size = self.hparams.get("vocab_size", len(tokenizer.vocab))
 513 |         assert max(tokenizer.vocab.values()) < vocab_size
 514 | 
 515 |         tokpre = self.get_vocab_base_pre(tokenizer)
 516 | 
 517 |         reverse_vocab = {id_: encoded_tok for encoded_tok, id_ in tokenizer.vocab.items()}
 518 |         added_vocab = tokenizer.get_added_vocab()
 519 | 
 520 |         for i in range(vocab_size):
 521 |             if i not in reverse_vocab:
 522 |                 tokens.append(f"[PAD{i}]")
 523 |                 toktypes.append(gguf.TokenType.UNUSED)
 524 |             else:
 525 |                 token: str = reverse_vocab[i]
 526 |                 if token in added_vocab:
 527 |                     if tokenizer.added_tokens_decoder[i].special or self.does_token_look_special(token):
 528 |                         toktypes.append(gguf.TokenType.CONTROL)
 529 |                     else:
 530 |                         token = token.replace(b"\xe2\x96\x81".decode("utf-8"), " ")  # pre-normalize user-defined spaces
 531 |                         toktypes.append(gguf.TokenType.USER_DEFINED)
 532 |                 else:
 533 |                     toktypes.append(gguf.TokenType.NORMAL)
 534 |                 tokens.append(token)
 535 | 
 536 |         return tokens, toktypes, tokpre
 537 | 
 538 |     # NOTE: this function is generated by convert_hf_to_gguf_update.py
 539 |     #       do not modify it manually!
 540 |     # ref:  https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/pull/6920
 541 |     # Marker: Start get_vocab_base_pre
 542 |     def get_vocab_base_pre(self, tokenizer) -> str:
 543 |         # encoding this string and hashing the resulting tokens would (hopefully) give us a unique identifier that
 544 |         # is specific for the BPE pre-tokenizer used by the model
 545 |         # we will use this unique identifier to write a "tokenizer.ggml.pre" entry in the GGUF file which we can
 546 |         # use in llama.cpp to implement the same pre-tokenizer
 547 | 
 548 |         chktxt = '\n \n\n \n\n\n \t \t\t \t\n  \n   \n    \n     \n🚀 (normal) 😶\u200d🌫️ (multiple emojis concatenated) ✅ 🦙🦙 3 33 333 3333 33333 333333 3333333 33333333 3.3 3..3 3...3 កាន់តែពិសេសអាច😁 ?我想在apple工作1314151天～ ------======= нещо на Български \'\'\'\'\'\'```````""""......!!!!!!?????? I\'ve been \'told he\'s there, \'RE you sure? \'M not sure I\'ll make it, \'D you like some tea? We\'Ve a\'lL'
 549 | 
 550 |         chktok = tokenizer.encode(chktxt)
 551 |         chkhsh = sha256(str(chktok).encode()).hexdigest()
 552 | 
 553 |         logger.debug(f"chktok: {chktok}")
 554 |         logger.debug(f"chkhsh: {chkhsh}")
 555 | 
 556 |         res = None
 557 | 
 558 |         # NOTE: if you get an error here, you need to update the convert_hf_to_gguf_update.py script
 559 |         #       or pull the latest version of the model from Huggingface
 560 |         #       don't edit the hashes manually!
 561 |         if chkhsh == "0ef9807a4087ebef797fc749390439009c3b9eda9ad1a097abbe738f486c01e5":
 562 |             # ref: https://huggingface.co/meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
 563 |             res = "llama-bpe"
 564 |         if chkhsh == "049ecf7629871e3041641907f3de7c733e4dbfdc736f57d882ba0b0845599754":
 565 |             # ref: https://huggingface.co/deepseek-ai/deepseek-llm-7b-base
 566 |             res = "deepseek-llm"
 567 |         if chkhsh == "347715f544604f9118bb75ed199f68779f423cabb20db6de6f31b908d04d7821":
 568 |             # ref: https://huggingface.co/deepseek-ai/deepseek-coder-6.7b-base
 569 |             res = "deepseek-coder"
 570 |         if chkhsh == "8aeee3860c56296a157a1fe2fad249ec40aa59b1bb5709f4ade11c4e6fe652ed":
 571 |             # ref: https://huggingface.co/tiiuae/falcon-7b
 572 |             res = "falcon"
 573 |         if chkhsh == "0876d13b50744004aa9aeae05e7b0647eac9d801b5ba4668afc01e709c15e19f":
 574 |             # ref: https://huggingface.co/BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5

 583 |             # ref: https://huggingface.co/openai-community/gpt2
 584 |             res = "gpt-2"
 585 |         if chkhsh == "32d85c31273f8019248f2559fed492d929ea28b17e51d81d3bb36fff23ca72b3":

 587 |             res = "stablelm2"

 589 |             # ref: https://huggingface.co/smallcloudai/Refact-1_6-base
 590 |             res = "refact"

 664 |             logger.warning("**          - the pre-tokenization config has changed upstream")
 665 |             logger.warning("**          Check your model files and convert_hf_to_gguf_update.py and update them accordingly.")
 666 |             logger.warning("** ref:     https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/pull/6920")
 667 |             logger.warning("**")
 668 |             logger.warning(f"** chkhsh:  {chkhsh}")
 669 |             logger.warning("**************************************************************************************")
 670 |             logger.warning("\n")
 671 |             raise NotImplementedError("BPE pre-tokenizer was not recognized - update get_vocab_base_pre()")
